goals.title: Ship v1 private document drive
goals.outcome: A household admin can drop or upload supported documents, see processing status, preview or inspect extracted text, search source and main-language text, organize with tags and collections, retry failures, and keep originals immutable in configured document storage.

goals.title: Keep backend API contract stable and tested
goals.outcome: The Go API owns OpenAPI and exposes authenticated setup, auth, documents, upload, search, settings, tags, collections, user grants, ingestion, and job routes with stable IDs, explicit statuses, and no filesystem path leaks.

goals.title: Run ingestion and indexing worker reliably
goals.outcome: The always-on Go worker detects stable files, reports changes to the API, leases jobs, extracts/OCRs/translates/embeds/indexes text, records step outcomes, and writes generated artifacts outside the document directory.

goals.title: Build SDK-backed web console
goals.outcome: The Vite React TypeScript console uses the generated SDK to support first-run setup, login, document list/detail/preview/text/events, upload, search, settings, tags, collections, retry, and sign out.

goals.title: Package self-hosted deployment and configuration
goals.outcome: Docker Compose runs the API, worker, one-shot migrations, and Postgres pgvector with Immich-style env configuration, stable host mounts for documents/app data/DB data, and smoke-testable images.

goals.title: Keep access control local and simple
goals.outcome: The self-hosted instance has first-run admin setup, admin/user roles, API-enforced ACLs, direct document grants for v1, protected downloads/previews, and no filesystem path exposure.

goals.title: Maintain repo process and agent memory
goals.outcome: Agents use Maat tickets before material work, record decisions and evidence, keep generated contracts synchronized, run the right gates, and leave product and memory state understandable for handoff.

tickets.title: Verify v1 document-drive acceptance workflow
tickets.description: Prove the product acceptance path from docs/product-plan.md using real or smoke-quality fixtures: drop or upload supported documents, process them, search source and main-language text, inspect document detail, organize with tags/collections, and retry failures without mutating originals.
acceptance: A Dutch scanned PDF can be moved into the configured document directory and found by a Dutch query.
The same document can be found through the selected main language after translation.
A DOCX keeps its base format and can be searched, downloaded, and inspected through extracted text.
OCR, translation, or embedding failure leaves the document visible with retry/status information.
Evidence names the exact command, fixture, commit, or manual smoke used.

tickets.title: Maintain API foundation, auth, documents, uploads, jobs, and library
tickets.description: Keep apps/api as the backend owner for configuration, routing, OpenAPI, auth, documents, TUS uploads, search, settings, tags, collections, users/direct grants, ingestion file reports, and job lease/complete/fail routes.
acceptance: Route or response changes update apps/api/openapi.json and generated SDK when the frontend contract changes.
Protected API responses expose stable IDs and never filesystem paths.
Relevant unit, integration, and contract tests are added or updated for behavior changes.
make test-fast or a narrower justified command passes and is recorded as evidence.

tickets.title: Resolve processing status vocabulary and job timelines
tickets.description: Align the documented processing states with API, SDK, worker, and UI behavior so agents and users can rely on one status model and useful processing history.
acceptance: Document status values are intentionally chosen and reflected consistently in OpenAPI, SDK types, API responses, worker updates, and UI labels.
Job complete/fail updates preserve enough step/event history for document detail and troubleshooting.
Any migration or compatibility concern is documented before changing persisted values.
Contract tests cover the chosen status values and event visibility.

tickets.title: Maintain watcher registration and document lifecycle
tickets.description: Keep the worker reconciler and orchestrator responsible for scanning the configured document directory, waiting for stable writes, hashing files, registering stable files with the API, handling duplicates/changed paths/deletes, and enqueueing index jobs while TUS remains only an upload path.
acceptance: Partial writes do not enqueue jobs until size and mtime are stable.
Duplicate checksums, same-path changes, deleted files, unreadable files, and symlink/path safety are tested or intentionally documented.
Generated artifacts are written under app data and never inside the document directory.
Worker-to-API registration routes remain protected by worker/admin auth.

tickets.title: Complete extraction OCR translation embedding pipeline
tickets.description: Keep the worker processing pipeline able to extract text from supported formats, OCR scanned/text-poor PDFs with configured languages, chunk by page then length, detect language, translate chunks outside DRIVE_MAIN_LANGUAGE, embed original and translated chunks, and return records for full-text and vector indexing.
acceptance: TXT, RTF, DOCX, ODT, native PDF, and scanned/text-poor PDF behavior is covered by focused or heavy tests as appropriate.
Translation or embedding provider failure records partial step status and does not hide the document or block original-language search.
Artifacts include enough tool/version data to support future reprocessing decisions.
Heavy proof covers real OCR fixtures, pgvector ranking, DOCX, and scanned PDF smoke before this is marked complete.

tickets.title: Operate the Vite React document console
tickets.description: Maintain the web console in apps/web as a quiet operational UI for household document management, using Vite, React, TypeScript, Tailwind, OXC, lucide icons, shadcn-style components, and generated SDK-backed API helpers.
acceptance: The UI supports setup/login, document list, upload, search, detail with preview/text/events, settings, tags, collections, retry, and sign out.
Frontend API calls use packages/sdk generated functions or a thin local wrapper over them, not independent endpoint contracts.
Typecheck and OXC lint pass for frontend changes.
Responsive layout avoids overlapping text and keeps document workflows efficient.

tickets.title: Keep OpenAPI and Orval SDK synchronized
tickets.description: Preserve the backend-owned contract flow from apps/api/openapi.json through Orval-generated packages/sdk to apps/web, and keep route parameter and response conventions stable for agents and frontend code.
acceptance: Backend route or response changes update OpenAPI in apps/api.
Generated SDK in packages/sdk is regenerated when OpenAPI changes affect frontend consumers.
OpenAPI smoke verifies required public, protected, admin, upload, ingestion, and job routes.
Route params remain kebab-case and downloads/previews stay behind API authorization.

tickets.title: Maintain first-run admin and direct document grants
tickets.description: Keep access control aligned with docs/access-control-plan.md: first-run setup creates the initial admin, admins manage users and direct document grants, users see only granted documents, and ACLs are enforced at the API layer without filesystem permission tricks.
acceptance: No-user startup requires setup before normal login.
Protected routes reject unauthenticated requests and non-admins cannot manage users/settings/grants.
Direct document grants allow and revoke user access as expected.
Path traversal and direct-path guessing are rejected without leaking filesystem paths.

tickets.title: Smoke Docker Compose backend deployment
tickets.description: Keep deploy/docker-compose.yml, deploy/example.env, Dockerfiles, and Make targets aligned with the self-hosted backend stack: Postgres pgvector, one-shot migrate, API, and always-on worker with document/app-data mounts.
acceptance: make docker-smoke succeeds or any failure is captured with exact logs and follow-up work.
Compose startup order remains postgres healthy -> migrate complete -> api healthy -> worker.
Required env covers documents, app data, DB data, worker token, main language, OCR languages, and Postgres credentials.
Worker image includes Poppler and Tesseract language packages required by default OCR settings.

tickets.title: Follow repo gates and Maat operating loop
tickets.description: Use the repo and Maat processes consistently so agents can coordinate: claim/create tickets before material work, include descriptions and acceptance criteria, comment meaningful progress, complete only with evidence, and run the right validation before handoff.
acceptance: Before material work, Maat status and project state are inspected and a relevant ticket is claimed or created.
New Maat goals include outcomes and new tickets include descriptions plus acceptance criteria.
Code changes obey ownership boundaries, update OpenAPI/SDK when contracts change, and run make check-code-lines before commit.
Completion evidence names tests, commits, PRs, or exact manual verification.

tickets.title: Rebuild Driver Maat memory with structured fields
tickets.description: Recreate the Driver Maat goals, tickets, and status events after old Driver entries were deleted, using the current schema requirements for goal outcomes, ticket descriptions, ticket acceptance criteria, and searchable data comments.
acceptance: Driver has active goals with non-empty Outcome sections for product, backend, worker, frontend, deployment/configuration, access control, and repo process.
Driver has tickets with non-empty Description sections and acceptance bullets for the major workstreams from the repo docs.
Each workstream ticket has a comment with source docs, current repo paths, implementation status, and verification data where available.
maat validate passes after the rebuild.

tickets.title: Document goal and ticket agent orchestration
tickets.description: Add the requested operating model to persistent repo and Maat instructions: one goal-level agent judges and coordinates each goal, ticket-level agents do implementation work, completed ticket work returns to the goal agent for acceptance verification, and agents are closed after committed work.
acceptance: AGENTS.md documents the goal-agent and ticket-agent responsibilities.
Maat has an event/comment recording the orchestration rule and verification flow.
The workflow states that ticket agents must commit their work before goal-agent verification and shutdown.
No product code behavior changes are made.

events.summary: Data/status: docs/product-plan.md defines the v1 goal and acceptance criteria. Current repo has API, worker, web, generated SDK, deployment files, and tests. make test-fast passed on 2026-05-26, including Go tests, web/sdk typecheck, OXC lint, and OpenAPI smoke. Heavy/e2e product acceptance is still the proof gap.

Data/status: apps/api/internal/http/router.go currently exposes health, openapi.json, setup, auth/login, protected documents/text/events/preview/retry/settings/search/tags/collections, admin documents/tags/collections/users/settings/uploads, and worker ingestion/jobs routes. docs/backend-tasks.md says backend work must include tests where relevant. make test-fast passed on 2026-05-26.

Data/status: docs/backend-tasks.md calls for queued, processing, partial-ready, ready, failed, while current generated SDK exposes queued, indexed, failed and code uses indexed/deleted in several places. This ticket is intentionally open until the product/API vocabulary is reconciled or explicitly documented as different from the early docs.

Data/status: worker files under apps/worker/internal/reconcile and apps/worker/internal/orchestrate implement stable-file scanning, registration, deleted-file reporting, duplicate handling, and artifact-boundary checks. Tests include partial writes, duplicates, deleted documents, and keeping artifacts outside documents. make test-fast passed on 2026-05-26.

Data/status: apps/worker/internal/processing contains extraction, PDF, OCR fallback, chunking, language, translation, embedding, and pipeline modules. docs/ingestion-pipeline.md defines steps from stable-file discovery through ready. make test-fast passed processing tests on 2026-05-26; make test-heavy was not run in this rebuild pass.

Data/status: docs/frontend-plan.md defines Vite React TypeScript Tailwind OXC and v1 screens. apps/web/src/app/App.tsx and components.tsx currently cover setup/login, document list, upload, search, detail preview, settings, tags, collections, retry, and sign out. make test-fast passed package SDK and web typecheck plus oxlint on 2026-05-26.

Data/status: docs/openapi-orval-plan.md defines api source -> openapi.json -> orval -> packages/sdk -> apps/web. Current files include apps/api/openapi.json, apps/api/openapi.go, packages/sdk/src/generated/drive.ts, and scripts/openapi-smoke.sh. make test-fast reported OpenAPI smoke ok on 2026-05-26.

Data/status: API files include setup.go, auth.go, users.go, acl.go, memory_acl.go, pg_acl.go, and tests for setup, auth, ACL, users, and path safety. docs/access-control-plan.md marks directory grants and collection grants as later work; v1 uses direct document grants. make test-fast passed API tests on 2026-05-26.

Data/status: docs/deployment.md and docs/configuration-plan.md define Immich-style env configuration and backend deployment. Current files include deploy/docker-compose.yml, deploy/example.env, deploy/api.dockerfile, deploy/worker.dockerfile, and scripts/docker-smoke.sh. make test-fast passed, but make docker-smoke was not run during this rebuild pass.

Data/status: AGENTS.md now contains the current Maat snippet from maat initialize: before work run maat status and maat project show, create or claim tickets, require goal outcomes and ticket descriptions/acceptance, comment progress, complete with evidence, validate and sync when done. Repo fast gate is make test-fast; heavy evidence is make test-heavy.

Data/status: rebuilt from docs/product-plan.md, backend-tasks.md, ingestion-pipeline.md, frontend-plan.md, openapi-orval-plan.md, access-control-plan.md, configuration-plan.md, deployment.md, AGENTS.md, current app/package/deploy files, git log, and make test-fast output. Auto-pull, auto-commit, and auto-push were disabled during the later write phase to avoid storage index races; Driver Markdown will be committed explicitly after validation.

evidence: Rebuilt Driver Maat memory with 7 outcome-backed goals, 11 description/acceptance-backed tickets, data/status comments on each workstream, make test-fast passed on 2026-05-26, and maat validate passed: validated 129 files ok.

events.summary: Orchestration rule recorded: assign one goal-level agent per active Maat goal; the goal agent coordinates and judges acceptance; the goal agent spawns ticket-level agents for scoped ticket work; ticket agents report changed files, commits, tests, and evidence; the goal agent verifies ticket acceptance before Maat completion; after work is committed, verified, and recorded, close ticket agents and later close the goal agent when coordination is no longer needed.

Goal-judge briefs collected: product judge requires e2e/heavy proof for Dutch scanned PDF, translated search, DOCX/originals, failure visibility, tags/collections; backend judge flags status vocabulary consistency across docs/API/OpenAPI/SDK/worker/UI; worker judge requires heavy OCR/pgvector/DOCX/scanned-PDF proof and API status coordination; web judge requires SDK-backed UI checks and browser evidence; deployment judge requires make docker-smoke; access judge requires fresh auth/ACL/path-safety evidence. Process goal handled locally due agent limit.

evidence: Updated AGENTS.md with goal-agent/ticket-agent orchestration and committed product repo change 5d34662; recorded orchestration rule and six goal-judge brief summary events in Maat; maat validate passed with 520 files ok; git diff --check passed; make check-markdown-lines exited successfully with existing docs line-budget warning.

goals.title: Enable autonomous agent project management
goals.outcome: Agents can register projects, create actionable goals and tickets, claim work, leave progress notes, complete tickets with evidence, validate state, and sync storage without hand-editing Markdown.

goals.title: Improve initialize dogfood experience
goals.outcome: Agents can initialize Maat in a new product repo, understand exactly where the storage repo is, copy concise agent instructions, and recover from common storage, Git, and PATH setup failures without guessing.

goals.title: Resolve Orion first-run setup friction
goals.outcome: The first Orion-on-Maat setup path is clear, recoverable, and honest about local storage, Git upstream, auto-sync, index cache, and ticket state, so agents can use Maat without accidental data loss or misleading status.

goals.title: Keep search reliable across project state
goals.outcome: Agents can search projects, goals, tickets, and events reliably, with SQLite search when available and a Markdown fallback when local indexes are missing or stale.

goals.title: Reduce conflicts for many concurrent agents
goals.outcome: Multiple agents can add goals, tickets, comments, and completion evidence with minimal merge conflicts by writing object files and append-only events instead of shared mutable logs.

goals.title: Keep state rebuildable on every machine
goals.outcome: Maat project state remains plain Markdown in Git, while indexes, config, install metadata, and local caches can be rebuilt or repaired independently on each machine.

goals.title: Provide terminal and TUI state visibility
goals.outcome: Humans and agents can inspect project state quickly from the terminal, including project summaries, ticket lists, ticket details, and a TUI dashboard that is readable during daily work.

goals.title: Build a proper terminal UI for daily project navigation
goals.outcome: The TUI becomes a fast daily workspace for scanning projects, navigating tickets, seeing ownership/state, filtering work, and refreshing when Markdown state changes.

goals.title: Rebuild TUI refresh experience
goals.outcome: The Maat TUI refreshes project memory predictably, shows users when data is loading or stale, supports an explicit manual refresh, and preserves navigation context while recovering cleanly from storage or Git refresh failures.

goals.title: Build terminal app catalog storage
goals.outcome: Maat stores terminal app catalog apps, patterns, decisions, and review events as Markdown objects that validate cleanly and remain readable without generated indexes.

goals.title: Ship catalog CLI and search
goals.outcome: Humans and agents can list, inspect, validate, and search terminal app catalog entries from the Maat CLI with stable JSON output and Markdown-backed search.

goals.title: Refresh TUI around catalog discovery
goals.outcome: The TUI presents a keyboard-first project and catalog experience with scannable lists, Kanban/project boards, detail panes, no-color readability, and clear routes from observations to Maat tickets.

goals.title: Ship Tauri macOS desktop setup
goals.outcome: A downloaded, signed macOS Tauri app can install and verify the maat CLI during first-run setup, connect to a user-selected Git-backed storage repo, and leave users with a working desktop entry point that preserves the CLI as the product API.

goals.title: Build Tauri desktop workspace
goals.outcome: The macOS app provides native project, ticket, catalog, search, sync, and write workflows backed entirely by maat CLI JSON commands, with clear error handling, update behavior, and release hardening.

goals.title: Stop creating source-repo .maat caches
goals.outcome: Maat never writes .maat cache files into a product/source repository unless that repository is explicitly configured as the Maat storage repo.

goals.title: Keep index caches inside storage
goals.outcome: JSON and SQLite indexes continue to live under the configured storage repo and remain rebuildable, local, and excluded from storage commits.

tickets.title: Keep initialize setup document concise
tickets.description: Keep the generated initialize instructions focused on the daily agent loop and required command forms instead of long background documentation.
acceptance: The generated snippet includes setup, read, create or claim, comment, complete, validate, and sync commands.
The snippet avoids obsolete flags and fits comfortably in an agent instruction file.

tickets.title: Clarify auto-pull permission warnings
tickets.description: Make auto-pull failures clear when storage exists but the agent cannot perform network or Git operations, without masking the command result.
acceptance: Human output explains that the Markdown read or write still happened when only auto-pull failed.
Agent-use output emits a warning update with the Git error and storage path.

tickets.title: Show storage access hint on permission failures
tickets.description: Improve setup, initialize, and read-path failures so permission errors explain which storage path failed, why access was denied, and what the agent should try next.
acceptance: A denied read or write prints the failing path and a concrete remediation such as choosing a writable storage repo or granting access.
JSON and agent-use output include enough structured warning detail for an agent to decide whether to retry, ask for approval, or switch storage paths.

tickets.title: Maintain project registration with initialize and project link
tickets.description: Keep project linking reliable for existing and new repositories, including inferred project keys, display names, source paths, and remotes.
acceptance: Project registration creates or updates projects/<project-key>/project.md with key, display name, status, summary, and identity metadata.
Repeated project link or initialize runs are idempotent and do not duplicate project records.

tickets.title: Add setup doctor command for storage and Git checks
tickets.description: Add a diagnostic command that validates the configured storage directory, Git repository health, branch/upstream state, write permissions, and index rebuildability.
acceptance: The command reports pass, warning, or fail for storage path, Git repo, upstream, writable project directories, and local index state.
The command exits non-zero only for conditions that prevent reliable Maat use.

tickets.title: Expose binary version context during initialize
tickets.description: Include the running Maat binary version and commit context in initialize/setup output so agents can tell whether local instructions match the installed CLI.
acceptance: Initialize output shows the Maat version and commit/date when available.
The generated instructions remain stable enough to paste into AGENTS.md without noisy machine-specific details.

tickets.title: Maintain claim comment and completion workflow with evidence
tickets.description: Keep the ticket lifecycle usable for agents by supporting claims, factual comments, and completion with evidence.
acceptance: Claims record actor and expiration as append-only events.
Completion requires evidence and records it in a ticket.completed event.

tickets.title: Keep SQLite local and rebuildable per agent
tickets.description: Ensure each agent can maintain its own SQLite cache without sharing it as authoritative state.
acceptance: SQLite indexes are regenerated from Markdown and can be deleted safely.
Storage workflows do not require committing local SQLite cache changes.

tickets.title: Make remote storage pushes explicit and intentional
tickets.description: Keep automatic remote pushes opt-in so agents do not publish state unexpectedly.
acceptance: Auto-push remains configurable and off when requested.
Commands that push require explicit configuration or an explicit push flag.

tickets.title: Keep search results usable for humans and JSON agents
tickets.description: Keep search output concise for humans and structured for agents.
acceptance: Human search output is scan-friendly and avoids dumping entire files.
JSON search output contains stable fields for type, path, line, title, and excerpt.

tickets.title: Maintain goal and ticket creation commands
tickets.description: Keep goal and ticket creation commands strict enough that new work always has an outcome, a description, and at least one acceptance criterion.
acceptance: Goal creation rejects missing outcomes.
Ticket creation rejects missing descriptions and missing acceptance criteria.

tickets.title: Maintain Markdown search fallback
tickets.description: Keep search useful when SQLite is unavailable, stale, or cannot be opened.
acceptance: maat search falls back to scanning Markdown files when the SQLite index cannot be used.
Fallback results remain readable and include enough context to open the matched file.

tickets.title: Remember installed binary path for bare uninstall
tickets.description: Record the binary name, install directory, and target path during successful install/update so future bare uninstall commands remove the same binary.
acceptance: Successful update or install script runs persist the binary location in local Maat config.
Bare maat uninstall uses the remembered location when --install-dir and --binary-name are omitted, and clears stale install metadata after removal.

tickets.title: Keep index failure warnings from causing duplicate writes
tickets.description: Make write commands preserve successful Markdown writes even when index refresh fails, and warn agents not to repeat the write.
acceptance: A write that succeeds but fails index refresh reports the warning clearly.
Agent-use output includes a machine-readable index warning so agents can rebuild later instead of duplicating objects.

tickets.title: Keep Markdown in Git as the only source of truth
tickets.description: Prevent hidden databases or generated caches from becoming authoritative project state.
acceptance: All project, goal, ticket, and event facts are represented in Markdown files under projects/.
SQLite and JSON indexes are documented and treated as rebuildable caches.

tickets.title: Avoid false uninstall progress for missing binaries
tickets.description: Fix uninstall output so users are not told that Maat is removing a binary when the configured target does not exist.
acceptance: When the target binary is missing, human output shows only the missing-binary warning and the target path.
The behavior is covered by a CLI regression test.

tickets.title: Maintain Bubble Tea project dashboard
tickets.description: Keep the TUI as a terminal-native dashboard for scanning projects and active work.
acceptance: maat tui launches without corrupting terminal state.
The dashboard shows project and ticket state from the configured storage repo.

tickets.title: Preserve object-per-file storage layout
tickets.description: Keep projects, goals, tickets, and events as separate files so concurrent writes usually touch different paths.
acceptance: New goals and tickets are written as individual files under their project directory.
Generated views are treated as rebuildable and not required for coordination.

tickets.title: Maintain status projects and project show commands
tickets.description: Keep summary commands accurate for project counts, goal counts, ticket counts, and detailed project metadata.
acceptance: maat status reports project, goal, ticket, active, done, and blocked counts accurately.
maat project show returns readable human output and JSON with project metadata.

tickets.title: Keep runtime SQLite cache files out of tracked storage
tickets.description: Ensure local SQLite/index cache files are not accidentally treated as project truth.
acceptance: Documentation and tooling identify .maat cache files as rebuildable.
Workflows avoid committing cache changes unless explicitly required.

tickets.title: Maintain ticket list and ticket show commands
tickets.description: Keep ticket list and detail views useful for deciding what to work on next.
acceptance: maat ticket list can filter by project and returns open ticket metadata in human and JSON modes.
maat ticket show includes description, acceptance criteria, status, goal, and project references.

tickets.title: Warn when storage is outside the active workspace
tickets.description: Make it clear when configured storage is outside the current product repo so agents know product changes and Maat state changes live in separate Git repositories.
acceptance: Relevant setup or initialize output names the storage repo path.
Instructions tell agents not to commit storage state into the product repo.

tickets.title: Keep human output colored concise and informative
tickets.description: Keep command-line output readable for humans while preserving JSON and agent-use output for automation.
acceptance: Human output uses concise labels and color only when enabled.
JSON output remains machine-readable and free of progress prose.

tickets.title: Maintain install update and uninstall flows
tickets.description: Keep Maat install, update, and uninstall behavior reliable across source installs and release downloads.
acceptance: maat update installs from a source path or release asset and reports the target path.
maat uninstall can remove the installed binary without deleting project storage.

tickets.title: Separate local memory commit status from remote push status
tickets.description: Make CLI output distinguish local storage commits from successful remote pushes.
acceptance: Sync output reports committed and pushed states separately.
Agent-use output exposes separate fields or warnings for commit and push results.

tickets.title: End initialize with a short next-step summary
tickets.description: Finish initialize with a compact checklist of the next commands an agent should run.
acceptance: The summary includes status, ticket list, goal create, ticket create, complete, validate, and sync examples.
The summary avoids long background explanation.

tickets.title: Keep high-frequency writes append-only through events
tickets.description: Keep comments, claims, and completions as append-only event files to avoid rewriting shared ticket bodies for routine progress updates.
acceptance: Ticket comments create ticket.commented events.
Claims and completions create separate event files with object references.

tickets.title: Maintain validation and sync after agent writes
tickets.description: Ensure agents can validate storage and sync changes after writing project state.
acceptance: maat validate catches malformed project, goal, ticket, and event files.
maat sync can report status, commit completed state, and push when explicitly requested.

tickets.title: Detect storage repos with commits but no upstream branch
tickets.description: Detect when the storage repo has local commits but no configured upstream so agents do not assume state has been shared.
acceptance: Sync status reports when a branch has no upstream.
Push guidance distinguishes between local commit success and remote synchronization.

tickets.title: Maintain JSON and SQLite index rebuild commands
tickets.description: Keep local indexes rebuildable from Markdown so search and dashboards can recover from missing or stale cache files.
acceptance: maat index rebuild regenerates JSON and SQLite indexes from project Markdown.
Deleting local index files does not destroy canonical project state.

tickets.title: Maintain SQLite search over projects goals tickets and events
tickets.description: Keep indexed search covering all primary object types and their important text fields.
acceptance: Search results include project, goal, ticket, and event hits with path, title, line, and excerpt.
Index rebuild populates SQLite from Markdown without requiring external services.

tickets.title: Require actionable details on created goals and tickets
tickets.description: Enforce and document the requirement that all new goals and tickets contain enough context for another agent to continue the work without reading the conversation that created them.
acceptance: Goal files include an Outcome section with a concrete result.
Ticket files include Description and Acceptance sections with actionable, testable criteria.

tickets.title: Maintain setup config for storage path and auto-sync defaults
tickets.description: Keep local setup config responsible for storage path, actor, and sync preferences without becoming the source of project truth.
acceptance: maat setup persists storage path, default actor, auto-pull, auto-commit, and auto-push preferences.
Project state remains in the configured storage repo, not in the local config file.

tickets.title: Detect ambiguous storage upstream and rebase configuration
tickets.description: Improve diagnostics for storage Git repositories whose remote, upstream, or pull-rebase settings make sync behavior unclear.
acceptance: Doctor or sync status reports upstream branch, ahead/behind state, and pull strategy warnings.
The guidance tells agents when to ask before pushing.

tickets.title: Improve TUI visual hierarchy and polish
tickets.description: Refine spacing, colors, labels, and empty states so the TUI feels calm and usable for repeated daily work.
acceptance: The TUI remains readable with color disabled.
Empty and error states tell the user what to do next.

tickets.title: Make the TUI refresh when Maat state changes
tickets.description: Refresh TUI data when project Markdown changes so long-running dashboard sessions reflect new goals, tickets, comments, and completions.
acceptance: The TUI can reload state without restarting the process.
Refresh failures are shown as recoverable UI errors instead of crashing.

tickets.title: Support project and ticket filtering in the TUI
tickets.description: Allow users to narrow the TUI by project, status, text query, or ownership.
acceptance: Filtering updates the visible ticket list without losing the selected context unnecessarily.
The active filter state is visible and easy to clear.

tickets.title: Show ticket state and ownership clearly in the TUI
tickets.description: Display ticket status, goal linkage, current claim or owner, and age/update context where available.
acceptance: A user can tell which tickets are active, waiting, claimed, or done without opening files.
Claim expiration or ownership metadata is visible when present.

tickets.title: Add a storage setup repair command
tickets.description: Provide a guided way to repair common storage setup issues such as missing config, unwritable paths, missing Git repo, no upstream, or stale indexes.
acceptance: The command reports what it can fix automatically and what requires user approval.
It does not delete project history or rewrite events.

tickets.title: Add focused ticket detail and action panes
tickets.description: Add a detail pane that shows description, acceptance criteria, recent events, and available actions for the selected ticket.
acceptance: The detail pane includes the selected ticket title, description, and acceptance criteria.
Actions are discoverable without cluttering the main list.

tickets.title: Design a column-based ticket board for the TUI
tickets.description: Design and implement a terminal layout that groups tickets by useful status columns while preserving compact project context.
acceptance: Open, waiting, and done or recently completed work are visually distinct.
The layout remains readable at common terminal sizes.

tickets.title: Make ticket navigation fast and keyboard-first
tickets.description: Support keyboard navigation for moving between projects, tickets, and detail panes.
acceptance: Users can move selection without typing commands.
The selected ticket remains visible and stable during navigation.

tickets.title: Print compact ticket state transitions
tickets.description: Make ticket claim, comment, and complete output easy to scan during agent runs.
acceptance: Human output names the ticket ID and resulting action.
JSON output includes action, project key, ticket ID, and event ID.

tickets.title: Add explicit TUI refresh state and manual reload
tickets.description: Rebuild the TUI refresh loop so users can trigger reloads explicitly, see when a refresh is in progress, and keep their selected project/mode stable after reloads.
acceptance: Pressing r in the TUI schedules an immediate dashboard reload without waiting for the next automatic tick.
The TUI view shows a concise refresh/loading indicator while a reload is in flight and keeps existing data visible.
Successful and failed reloads preserve project selection and detail mode, with tests covering the behavior.

tickets.title: Prevent overlapping TUI refresh races
tickets.description: Add refresh generation or in-flight protection so slow dashboard loads cannot overlap indefinitely or let older refresh results overwrite newer state.
acceptance: Automatic refresh ticks do not start another load while a refresh is already in flight.
Late refresh results cannot move the dashboard backward or clobber a newer successful reload.
Unit tests cover in-flight tick behavior and stale result handling.

tickets.title: Refresh TUI with pull-aware first load
tickets.description: Make the initial TUI screen use the same pull-aware loader as later auto-refreshes when auto-pull is enabled, so the first view is not stale for several seconds.
acceptance: RunTUIWithOptions uses the pull-aware dashboard loader before first render when AutoPullBeforeRefresh is true.
Pull warnings on initial load are surfaced without hiding successfully loaded dashboard data.
Tests cover loader selection for initial load and refresh load.

tickets.title: Define catalog Markdown schema
tickets.description: Document the Markdown object shapes for catalog apps, reusable patterns, decisions, opportunities, and catalog review events under projects/maat/catalog. Include required fields, stable IDs, links between patterns and Maat work, and plain-Markdown examples.
acceptance: docs/schema.md or a dedicated catalog schema doc defines required fields for apps, patterns, decisions, opportunities, and events.
The documented paths match projects/maat/catalog/apps, patterns, decisions, opportunities, and events.
At least one complete example app and one complete pattern are readable without generated indexes.

tickets.title: Implement catalog loader and validation
tickets.description: Add Go types and loading/validation logic for catalog app, pattern, decision, and opportunity Markdown files. Validation should catch missing IDs, malformed links, duplicate slugs, invalid decision states, and broken references to goals or tickets when those links are present.
acceptance: Catalog loader returns typed objects from Markdown files under projects/<project-key>/catalog.
Validation reports missing required fields, duplicate object IDs or slugs, invalid states, and broken object links.
Unit tests cover valid fixtures and at least three malformed catalog objects.

tickets.title: Seed first catalog observations
tickets.description: Create initial catalog fixtures or sample objects from the IA: lazygit, btop, gh-dash, and superfile as apps; focused detail pane, keyboard model, background refresh, and empty states as patterns. Keep metadata honest when unknown by marking it unknown instead of inventing values.
acceptance: Initial catalog objects exist as plain Markdown and validate successfully.
Each seeded app has name, slug, summary, source link, category, tags, and last reviewed date.
Each seeded pattern explains the problem solved, observed apps, Maat relevance, and at least one related goal or ticket link where known.

tickets.title: Add catalog list and show commands
tickets.description: Add maat catalog list and maat catalog show commands for humans. List should support apps, patterns, decisions, and opportunities with compact scannable columns. Show should render a structured detail view for one object.
acceptance: maat catalog list apps shows app name, summary, language, license, category, and review date when present.
maat catalog list patterns shows title, category, observed apps, and related Maat work.
maat catalog show <id-or-slug> renders summary, metadata, links, notes, related patterns, and related goals or tickets.

tickets.title: Expose catalog JSON for agents
tickets.description: Add --json output for catalog list and show so agents can consume stable catalog object IDs, linked source apps, linked patterns, related goals, related tickets, and decision states without scraping human text.
acceptance: catalog list --json emits stable arrays for apps, patterns, decisions, and opportunities.
catalog show --json emits one typed object with stable ID or slug, object kind, metadata, links, and related Maat work.
JSON tests assert field names for agent-facing IDs and links remain stable.

tickets.title: Index catalog objects for search
tickets.description: Extend the existing Markdown and SQLite search paths so catalog apps, patterns, decisions, opportunities, and review events are searchable alongside projects, goals, tickets, and events.
acceptance: maat search can find catalog app summaries and notes.
maat search can find catalog pattern problem statements, Maat relevance, and related ticket IDs.
SQLite rebuild includes catalog objects without making SQLite authoritative.
Search tests cover catalog objects through both direct Markdown fallback and SQLite-backed search where applicable.

tickets.title: Add catalog mode to the TUI
tickets.description: Add a TUI mode based on docs/terminal-apps-ia.md with apps, patterns, and detail panes. It should support apps, patterns, decisions, and opportunities modes while keeping keyboard navigation fast and predictable.
acceptance: The TUI exposes a catalog mode reachable from the normal dashboard without hiding the project list flow.
Apps and patterns can be scanned in adjacent panes with a detail pane for the selected object.
tab switches panes or modes, / searches, f filters, enter inspects, r refreshes, and q quits.

tickets.title: Polish project board detail flow
tickets.description: Refine the primary TUI path so a human can see all projects, open one into a Kanban-style board, move through board items, and read a focused detail view with title, description, acceptance criteria, status, ownership, related goal, and recent events.
acceptance: Project list remains the first TUI screen for project-management use.
Opening a project leads to a board-like ticket view, and opening a ticket leads to a readable detail view.
The ticket detail includes description, acceptance criteria, current status, owner or claim information, related goal, and recent comments or completion evidence when available.
Back navigation returns from detail to board and from board to project list.

tickets.title: Verify keyboard and no-color readability
tickets.description: Add focused verification for the refreshed TUI at realistic terminal widths and no-color output. The checks should cover project list, project board, ticket detail, catalog apps, catalog patterns, empty states, and filtered states.
acceptance: TUI tests or golden snapshots cover narrow, medium, and wide terminal widths.
No-color output remains readable and does not depend on color alone for selected, status, or empty-state meaning.
Keyboard-only paths for project board detail and catalog inspection are documented and tested.
go test ./internal/maatui and go test ./... pass after the refresh.

tickets.title: Scaffold the Tauri app shell
tickets.description: Create the initial Tauri macOS app structure for Maat with a native window, app lifecycle hooks, settings storage, and a minimal command-runner bridge capable of invoking a configured maat binary.
acceptance: A Tauri app launches on macOS from the source tree.
The app can run maat version --json through a command runner and display or log the parsed result.
The command runner captures stdout, stderr, exit status, and structured JSON without scraping human output.

tickets.title: Build the first-run storage setup assistant
tickets.description: Create the desktop setup flow for selecting, creating, or cloning a Git-backed Maat storage repository, then configure the CLI with maat setup and validate it with setup doctor.
acceptance: A user can create a new local storage repo, select an existing repo, or enter a clone URL when clone support is enabled.
The app runs maat setup --storage <path> --actor <name> --json after storage selection.
The app runs maat setup doctor --storage <path> --fix --json and shows blocking checks, warnings, and safe fixes.

tickets.title: Bundle and install the app-private CLI
tickets.description: Package a signed maat binary inside the macOS app and install or update an app-private copy under Application Support during first-run setup.
acceptance: The app bundle includes the correct darwin maat binary for the target architecture.
On first launch, the bundled binary can install or refresh ~/Library/Application Support/maat/bin/maat using maat update --source ... --json.
The app records the installed CLI path and verifies it with maat version --json before using it.

tickets.title: Keep first-run sync choices conservative
tickets.description: Design setup defaults so the app does not add remotes, push commits, or enable auto-push unless the user explicitly chooses those behaviors.
acceptance: Auto-push is not enabled by default in the desktop setup flow.
Adding a storage remote or pushing storage state requires a clear user action.
The setup flow communicates that Markdown in the storage Git repo remains the source of truth.

tickets.title: Add sync validation and index controls
tickets.description: Expose desktop controls for sync status, validate, push, and index rebuild so users can recover or publish state without leaving the app.
acceptance: The app can run maat sync --status --json and present ahead, behind, committed, and pushed state clearly.
The app can run maat validate --json and show validation issues with file paths and messages.
The app offers maat index rebuild when a stale or failed index warning is detected.

tickets.title: Map CLI errors to recoverable UI states
tickets.description: Turn common CLI failures and setup doctor checks into specific desktop states instead of generic error dialogs.
acceptance: Missing CLI, missing setup, invalid storage, Git credential failures, index failures, and merge conflicts each have distinct UI handling.
CLI stderr and JSON details remain available for troubleshooting.
A successful Markdown write followed by an index failure is shown as durable state with a rebuild option, not as a failed write retry.

tickets.title: Add desktop write actions through the CLI
tickets.description: Add native actions for creating goals and tickets, claiming tickets, commenting, completing with evidence, and syncing, while routing every state mutation through the maat CLI.
acceptance: Goal creation uses maat goal create with an outcome and shows the created ID.
Ticket creation enforces description and acceptance criteria before invoking maat ticket create.
Claim, comment, and complete actions use the existing ticket CLI commands and refresh visible state after success.

tickets.title: Harden signing notarization and permissions
tickets.description: Prepare the macOS app for trusted distribution with signed app and CLI binaries, notarization, limited filesystem access, and no credential storage.
acceptance: The Tauri app and bundled maat binary are signed for macOS distribution.
The app is notarized or has a documented notarization path.
The app only requests storage repo access when the user selects a repo and relies on existing Git credential helpers instead of storing credentials.

tickets.title: Resolve first-release desktop product decisions
tickets.description: Close the open product decisions from docs/macos-app-architecture.md before implementation begins in earnest.
acceptance: The team decides whether first release installs only the app-private CLI or also prompts for terminal CLI installation.
The team decides whether clone-storage support ships in the first release or starts with selecting/creating local storage.
The team decides whether auto-push is exposed in first-run setup or only advanced settings.
The team decides whether to embed a read-only terminal/TUI view or focus only on native CLI-backed views.

tickets.title: Define app and CLI update behavior
tickets.description: Implement the update model for a Tauri app that ships with a bundled CLI while still allowing the app to know and verify the CLI it is using.
acceptance: App updates can replace the bundled CLI and refresh the app-private installed CLI.
The app records and displays the active CLI path and version.
Advanced CLI self-update behavior through maat update --json is either implemented or explicitly deferred.

tickets.title: Add optional terminal CLI installation
tickets.description: Offer a setup or settings action that installs the same maat CLI for terminal use without making shell profile changes mandatory for the desktop app.
acceptance: The desktop app remains usable with only the app-private CLI installed.
A user can explicitly install or link maat into a PATH directory for terminal use.
The UI explains the app-private CLI path and terminal CLI path as separate install targets.

tickets.title: Build read-only desktop dashboard views
tickets.description: Implement the first native desktop views for project status, project list, project details, tickets, catalog objects, and search using existing maat CLI JSON commands.
acceptance: The dashboard loads maat status --json and maat projects --json through the command runner.
Project and ticket detail views use maat project show, ticket list, and ticket show JSON output.
Catalog and search views use maat catalog list/show and maat search JSON output where available.

tickets.title: Remove cwd fallback from normal storage resolution
tickets.description: Change storage resolution so normal commands use explicit --storage or configured storage and otherwise fail instead of treating the current working directory as storage.
acceptance: Commands run from a source repo without --storage and without configured storage return a clear setup error.
No command in this path creates ./ .maat or ./ .maat/index.sqlite in the source repo.
Existing configured-storage use still works without passing --storage.

tickets.title: Preserve setup and initialize ergonomics
tickets.description: Keep first-run setup usable while making initialize and project link refuse ambiguous storage resolution from source repositories.
acceptance: maat setup can still prompt for or accept an absolute Git-backed storage repo.
maat initialize and maat project link require configured storage or explicit --storage before writing.
Error text tells the user to run maat setup --storage /path/to/storage when storage is missing.

tickets.title: Add regression tests for source repo cache creation
tickets.description: Cover the exact regression where search, index rebuild, doctor fix, initialize, or write flows create .maat in the product repo because storage fell back to cwd.
acceptance: A test runs a representative read/search command from a non-storage source repo with missing config and asserts no .maat directory is created.
A test runs the same command with configured storage and asserts the cache lands under configured storage/.maat.
The test suite covers both human and JSON error paths where practical.

tickets.title: Guard index writes to resolved storage
tickets.description: Make index refresh code write only under the already resolved storage root and fail loudly when storage resolution is absent or ambiguous.
acceptance: WriteIndex and SQLite rebuild callers receive an explicit resolved storage path.
Index refresh never independently falls back to cwd.
Failures report that indexes are rebuildable and identify the intended storage path when available.

tickets.title: Document configured storage and cache boundaries
tickets.description: Update user and agent documentation so configured storage is the normal operating mode and cache files are described as storage-local rebuildable artifacts.
acceptance: README and install/search docs explain that normal commands use configured storage after setup.
Docs state that <storage>/.maat/index.json and <storage>/.maat/index.sqlite are rebuildable and not committed.
Docs no longer imply source repos may safely receive .maat caches during normal use.

tickets.title: Decide and enforce storage cache ignore policy
tickets.description: Decide whether storage-local .maat cache files are only excluded by Maat sync pathspecs or also ignored by the storage repo itself, then make tooling and docs match that decision.
acceptance: The project explicitly decides whether setup should create or maintain a storage .gitignore entry for .maat/.
If .gitignore enforcement is chosen, setup or setup doctor creates or validates the ignore entry without touching product repos.
If pathspec-only exclusion is chosen, docs and tests state that normal Git commands can still see .maat as local untracked or modified cache files.

Remaining work: replace the raw path entry with native create/select storage flows and add richer doctor check rendering.

Product commit a0f7bfa adds app-private CLI installation from a selected binary path but does not yet install or link the terminal-facing maat command into a PATH directory. Keep this ticket open for the explicit terminal CLI install action and path health checks.

Product commit a0f7bfa implements status, project, ticket, and search desktop views backed by CLI JSON. Remaining work before completion: add catalog list/show UI and fuller project/ticket detail view models matching docs/desktop-dashboard-views.md.

Product commit a0f7bfa implements ticket create, claim, comment, and complete actions through CLI JSON. Remaining work before completion: add goal creation and tighten form validation/error mapping for required description and acceptance criteria.

Product commit a0f7bfa preserves stdout, stderr, exit code, duration, and CLI path from the Rust command runner and handles missing Tauri bridge in browser preview. Remaining work before completion: map missing CLI, missing setup, invalid storage, Git credentials, index warnings, and merge conflicts to distinct UI states.

Product commit a0f7bfa records active CLI path/version and supports refreshing an app-private CLI from a selected binary path. Remaining work before completion: wire bundled CLI refresh into app update flow and decide or implement advanced maat update --json behavior.

Product commit a0f7bfa builds an unsigned local Maat.app bundle and keeps storage access behind explicit user-provided paths through CLI commands. Remaining work before completion: real icon assets, code signing identity, notarization path, bundled CLI signing, and permission review.

Reviewer synthesis: core change is storage resolution, not project inference. loadStoreAndRest must stop falling through to cwd when config is missing, empty, malformed, or unreadable. Keep cwd-based linked-project inference only after storage is already resolved. Acceptance should cover goal create, ticket create, search, and index rebuild without configured storage failing before dot-maat is created; configured storage from arbitrary cwd; and explicit storage plus project inference.

Reviewer synthesis: setup ergonomics also includes setup prompt defaults and setup rules, not only initialize and project link. Missing config from a Git source repo must not create projects, setup.md, or dot-maat in cwd. Decide whether setup may default to cwd at all; if it can, require explicit confirmation that the current directory is intended storage.

Reviewer synthesis: one representative command is under-scoped. Cover every index-writing route or at least the highest-risk paths: search with missing config, search with configured storage from source cwd, index rebuild with missing config, sync without status, project link, initialize, and one write mutation. Include human, JSON, and agent-use error paths where practical, and assert both source cwd has no dot-maat and storage dot-maat exists when indexing is expected.

Reviewer synthesis: docs need to say dot-maat belongs under configured storage, not product repos during normal use. Update storage-model wording, macOS app architecture command examples that omit explicit storage, README/install/search-index cache descriptions to say cache files are not committed, setup docs to state bare commands use configured storage after setup, and architecture wording that overstates SQLite as general fast reads.

Reviewer synthesis: lower-level index guards cannot be the policy boundary because WriteIndex and RebuildSQLiteIndex only receive paths and cannot know whether a path came from cwd fallback. Make command-level resolved storage the boundary. Lower-level APIs can still reject empty or non-absolute stores as invariant checks, and the ticket should explicitly decide whether custom SQLite Path is test-only or supported public behavior.

Reviewer synthesis: this ticket exists because Maat sync pathspec exclusion is not the same guarantee as storage repo ignore rules. Decide explicitly whether dot-maat should remain visible to plain Git status or be ignored by setup and doctor. Tie the decision back to G-20260529-141731-daf6 because cache placement depends on strict storage resolution.

goals.title: Adopt Maat project memory
goals.outcome: Orion agents consistently use Maat as canonical project memory, with AGENTS.md containing the current Maat daily loop and required goal, ticket, validation, and sync commands.

goals.title: Make health status explainable and trustworthy
goals.outcome: Users can tell whether an Orion status is caused by agent availability, monitor rollup behavior, stale data, missing reports, or monitor check failures.

goals.title: Expose raw report context for debugging
goals.outcome: Operators can inspect the actual report payload and useful check metadata behind agent reports and monitor history without guessing from summary badges.

goals.title: Add incident lifecycle controls
goals.outcome: Operators can intentionally acknowledge or resolve incidents from the browser, and removed or disabled monitors do not leave confusing stuck incidents behind.

goals.title: Add Core runtime diagnostics and ingestion metrics
goals.outcome: Core exposes enough runtime diagnostics to tell whether report ingestion, incident reconciliation, database work, and slow requests are healthy without relying on an external monitoring stack.

goals.title: Harden Agent local state durability
goals.outcome: The Agent can preserve important local operational state across restarts and outages when real usage shows in-memory retry behavior is not enough.

goals.title: Improve alert operations
goals.outcome: Alert channels can be tested and diagnosed intentionally, and non-critical notification delivery work no longer risks slowing Agent report ingestion.

goals.title: Design status page architecture
goals.outcome: Orion has a concrete architectural plan for creating, configuring, and publishing status pages

goals.title: Prove the first real self-hosted deployment
goals.outcome: A real Core and at least one real Agent are deployed and verified end to end, with failures recorded as follow-up work instead of lingering in planning docs.

goals.title: Add Console browser and visual smoke coverage
goals.outcome: Console flows have lightweight automated browser coverage after the manual flows stabilize, reducing reliance on visual-only QA for regressions.

goals.title: Collect and expose service logs
goals.outcome: Operators can distinguish Orion operational events from service and file logs, then retrieve useful Agent/Core log context for later diagnosis.

goals.title: Design Core-managed monitors
goals.outcome: Orion has a reviewed product and implementation plan for monitors registered in Console and executed by Core instead of an Agent

goals.title: Automate data lifecycle scheduling
goals.outcome: Core automatically runs configured archive and rollup lifecycle work on schedule while preserving manual controls and observable last-run metadata.

goals.title: Establish continuous verification coverage
goals.outcome: The repository has a normal CI baseline for Core, Agent, Console, and generated contract checks so regressions are caught continuously instead of only during manual release passes.

goals.title: M1: Core owner, worker app, and HTTP MVP
goals.outcome: A Core-owned HTTP monitor can be created by API, claimed and executed by the Core monitor worker, reported into monitor_reports, and shown in the existing monitor list.

goals.title: M2: Console creation workflow
goals.outcome: A user can create, test, pause, resume, edit, delete, filter, and inspect Core monitors from Console without editing Agent YAML.

goals.title: Make current alerts honest
goals.outcome: Console and Core present the existing alert system accurately, including webhook and email destinations, test actions, expanded delivery logs, and visible global alert rules.

goals.title: Add reusable SMTP alert services
goals.outcome: Orion supports reusable SMTP service configuration and email destinations with secret-safe API responses, connection testing, and legacy email-channel compatibility.

goals.title: M3: Heartbeats
goals.outcome: Core supports cron, backup, script, and scheduled-job monitoring through generated heartbeat endpoints and worker-side missed-heartbeat reconciliation.

goals.title: Add explicit alert routes
goals.outcome: Alert delivery decisions are controlled by explicit routes with event, severity, owner, maintenance, destination, and dry-run behavior instead of implicit channel subscriptions.

goals.title: M4: Better incident controls
goals.outcome: Core monitors have enough noise controls to be useful in production without opening incidents for short transient failures.

goals.title: Add alert grouping policies
goals.outcome: Orion can group related alert events by incident, monitor, service label, owner, or severity, including delayed summaries and visible grouped or suppressed delivery history.

goals.title: Queue and retry alert delivery
goals.outcome: Alert delivery runs through durable delivery and attempt records with retry scheduling, backoff, worker execution, and transport-specific failure stages for SMTP and webhooks.

goals.title: M5: Components and status page groundwork
goals.outcome: Core and Agent monitors can be mapped to service and status-page components so incidents identify impacted components and future status pages can consume monitor health.

goals.title: Add rich alert templates and integrations
goals.outcome: Orion supports versioned alert payloads, richer email templates, signing helpers, and additional notification integrations once core routing and grouping are stable.

goals.title: M6: Full monitor catalog expansion
goals.outcome: Orion implements the full now-scope monitor catalog through the Core monitor worker with clear safety limits, report shapes, and incident behavior.

goals.title: Ship manual public status pages
goals.outcome: Administrators can create, preview, and publish a public status page with safe component health, manual incidents, and scheduled maintenance

goals.title: Integrate status pages with incidents
goals.outcome: Internal incidents can be safely translated into public status page incidents with suggested affected components, public updates, resolution flow, and auditability

goals.title: Add status page uptime and history
goals.outcome: Published status pages show component uptime, incident history, and cached public history views derived from existing monitor uptime and public incident data

goals.title: Add status page subscriptions
goals.outcome: Visitors can subscribe to public status page updates through confirmed, rate-limited destinations and receive only public incident communications

goals.title: Customize public status pages
goals.outcome: Status pages can be branded and distributed through custom presentation options without weakening public/private data boundaries

goals.title: Resolve status page architecture decisions
goals.outcome: Open status page architecture decisions are documented before implementation choices become expensive to change

goals.title: Incident detail phase 1: lifecycle actions
goals.outcome: operators can acknowledge, manually resolve, mark covered, and reopen incidents from Console with Core-backed audit events and generated API contracts

goals.title: Incident detail phase 2: coverage semantics
goals.outcome: covered incidents prevent duplicate reopen noise for still-failing monitors while preserving a clear audit trail and recovery behavior

goals.title: Incident detail phase 3: evidence and debugging
goals.outcome: incident detail explains the failure with normalized trigger and latest-report evidence plus raw inspection paths across incident, monitor, and agent views

goals.title: Incident detail phase 4: operational analytics
goals.outcome: incident history becomes searchable and measurable for recurring failures, manual handling patterns, notification reliability, and time-to-acknowledge or resolve

goals.title: Harden Core monitor network target policy
goals.outcome: Core-managed monitors enforce an explicit network target policy that prevents unsafe SSRF-style access while preserving intentional self-hosted LAN monitoring when enabled

goals.title: Complete Core monitor catalog hardening
goals.outcome: Advanced Core monitor types are production-ready with type-specific validation and explicit runtime packaging for browser-based checks

goals.title: Improve domain expiration fallback coverage
goals.outcome: Domain expiration monitors report expiration data more reliably when RDAP is unavailable by using a deliberate fallback strategy

goals.title: Add Agent token lifecycle controls
goals.outcome: Agent authentication tokens can be rotated or revoked deliberately without orphaning healthy monitors or requiring unsafe manual database edits

goals.title: Add public subscriber privacy lifecycle
goals.outcome: Public status page subscribers have a clear privacy lifecycle beyond unsubscribe, including retention and deletion behavior when policy requires it

goals.title: Turn colleague feedback into product readiness plan
goals.outcome: an actionable documented backlog for README, examples, deployment, terminology, and planning hygiene feedback

goals.title: Seed status page demo data
goals.outcome: demo seed data includes representative status pages, components, mappings, public incidents, history, and subscriptions where supported

goals.title: Improve README evaluation clarity
goals.outcome: first-time evaluators can understand why Orion exists, how the components fit together, and how Docker-hosted Core should be used

goals.title: Add runnable first Orion example
goals.outcome: new users can run a tiny real service and see how Orion monitors it through Docker Compose

goals.title: Show project quality signals
goals.outcome: README gives evaluators visible, current test and coverage confidence signals

goals.title: Clean repository metadata noise
goals.outcome: macOS metadata files are removed from version control and prevented from coming back

goals.title: Clarify Kubernetes deployment posture
goals.outcome: deployment docs explain Docker-first usage, when Kubernetes makes sense, and what a future minikube example must prove

goals.title: Audit public planning hygiene
goals.outcome: public planning files and agent instructions are intentional, non-sensitive, and separated from private project memory

goals.title: Simplify alerting to webhook rules
goals.outcome: Orion alerting supports only generic webhook destinations, has editable rules with a flowchart Console setup, removes obsolete SMTP Slack and Discord alert functionality, and has E2E coverage proving the primary alert workflow.

goals.title: Core cleanup: inventory backend coverage
goals.outcome: Every Core backend route, service method, worker runner, migration, diagnostics path, and operational job is mapped to a coverage status and follow-up ticket.

goals.title: Core cleanup: split large status page API files
goals.outcome: Status page backend code is split by responsibility while preserving route registration, response shape, OpenAPI annotations, and existing behavior.

goals.title: Core cleanup: make API tests navigable
goals.outcome: Core API integration tests are split into feature-scoped files with shared helpers so backend behavior is easier to review and extend without changing runtime behavior.

goals.title: Core cleanup: fill high-risk backend test gaps
goals.outcome: High-risk Core backend behavior has focused automated coverage for auth, token lifecycle, target policy, public/private boundaries, alert side effects, migrations, and data lifecycle jobs.

goals.title: Core cleanup: extract monitor validation
goals.outcome: Core monitor kind normalization, per-kind validation, and redaction are isolated from lifecycle persistence so monitor catalog changes are easier to test and review.

goals.title: Core cleanup: enforce future backend coverage
goals.outcome: Future Core backend changes are blocked or explicitly tracked unless they include the right unit, service, API, worker, migration, or contract coverage.

goals.title: Core cleanup: add backend quality gates
goals.outcome: Core CI includes meaningful backend safety gates for tests, race checks, generated contracts, vulnerabilities, and Go modernization linting.

goals.title: Core cleanup: apply safe Go modernization
goals.outcome: Core owned non-generated Go code uses current idioms where they improve readability or safety, with behavior preserved and tests passing.

goals.title: Settings readiness: restore E2E trust
goals.outcome: Settings browser coverage is reliable, reaches the Settings page, and verifies read, update, validation, rollup, and archive flows in the smoke suite.

goals.title: Settings readiness: harden archive path security
goals.outcome: Data lifecycle archive paths are validated against a safe policy, covered by Core tests, and documented for self-hosted operators.

goals.title: Settings readiness: make manual maintenance safe
goals.outcome: Manual archive and rollup actions clearly communicate impact, prevent duplicate execution, and return useful operator-safe result states.

goals.title: Restore E2E confidence
goals.outcome: Console browser smoke coverage is green after the Server terminology migration and proves login, navigation, monitor, heartbeat, and incident lifecycle workflows against seeded Core data.

goals.title: Settings readiness: add lifecycle auditability
goals.outcome: Settings updates and manual lifecycle actions emit audit events with actor context and can be inspected from the operational logs surface.

goals.title: Settings readiness: improve lifecycle product UX
goals.outcome: The Settings page presents data lifecycle controls as an operator-ready workflow with clear sections, validation, and recent activity context.

goals.title: Finish Server terminology migration
goals.outcome: Product-facing copy, tests, docs, screenshots, and architecture notes consistently use Server while compatibility surfaces continue to use orion-agent and agent API names where required.

goals.title: Settings readiness: close planning and docs
goals.outcome: Settings readiness work is tracked, documented, and synchronized across Maat, architecture docs, and PR checklists without stale milestones.

goals.title: Harden public deployment security
goals.outcome: Public or production Core deployments require explicit admin authentication, document required secrets, and include regression coverage for secure frontend route behavior.

goals.title: Make first-run usefulness obvious
goals.outcome: The canonical first-run example demonstrates a real monitored service, a visible Server, monitor states, incident creation, and inspectable logs with clear reset instructions.

goals.title: Close product workflow gaps
goals.outcome: Operational Console workflows for monitors, alerts, status pages, and empty or error states are complete enough for repeated real use rather than demo-only seeded data.

goals.title: Define release readiness gate
goals.outcome: Release work is blocked on documented Core, Agent, Console, generated-contract, Docker, and browser-smoke verification gates, with known warnings classified as blockers or follow-ups.

goals.title: Status pages: prove end-to-end workflow
goals.outcome: status page creation, publication, public viewing, public incident updates, and privacy leak checks are covered by browser E2E tests

goals.title: Status pages: harden public data boundaries
goals.outcome: public status page JSON, HTML, feed, badge, history, and subscriber DTOs are protected by read-time sanitization and regression tests against internal data leaks

goals.title: Monitor readiness: make scope honest
goals.outcome: Orion's monitor documentation and product copy accurately describe first-release monitor scope and deferred monitor types.

goals.title: Status pages: finish subscriber operations
goals.outcome: operators can safely inspect and manage public status page subscribers and delivery records without exposing raw destinations or tokens

goals.title: Incident readiness: restore E2E coverage
goals.outcome: full Console browser smoke coverage is trusted again, including incident lifecycle coverage, with stale navigation selectors repaired

goals.title: Status pages: implement subscriber privacy lifecycle
goals.outcome: expired pending, unsubscribed, bounced, and erased public subscribers follow documented retention, anonymization, hard-delete, and audit behavior

goals.title: Monitor readiness: finish Console creation
goals.outcome: Console can create, edit, test, pause, resume, and delete the first-release Core monitor types with type-specific forms.

goals.title: Status pages: improve operator workflow
goals.outcome: the Console status page editor supports clear staged workflows for basics, components, incidents, subscribers, settings, validation, preview, publish, rollback, and deletion

goals.title: Incident readiness: structured lifecycle metadata
goals.outcome: incident lifecycle actions preserve actor, timing, note, and allowed-action state so operators can audit who changed what and why

goals.title: Status pages: reconcile release shape and docs
goals.outcome: status page docs, architecture decisions, API behavior, and Console behavior agree on first-release cardinality and shipped capabilities

goals.title: Monitor readiness: improve detail usefulness
goals.outcome: Monitor detail pages explain current state, failure cause, schedule, incident context, and configuration without forcing users into raw JSON first.

goals.title: Incident readiness: tighten payload security
goals.outcome: incident evidence and raw report inspection remain useful while redacting sensitive values across all monitor report payload types

goals.title: Status pages: make first release useful
goals.outcome: the status page feature clearly demonstrates operational value through demo data, preview usefulness, public uptime visibility, server mapping health, and publish-diff context

goals.title: Incident readiness: operational next actions
goals.outcome: incident detail directs operators to concrete fix paths such as notification retry, monitor tuning, related incidents, and status publication

goals.title: Monitor readiness: expose worker health
goals.outcome: Console visibly distinguishes Core API health from Core monitor worker health and warns when Core monitors cannot run.

goals.title: Monitor readiness: tighten monitor security
goals.outcome: Monitor admin flows, target policy behavior, and heartbeat boundaries are secure enough for exposed self-hosted deployments.

goals.title: Incident readiness: public status draft workflow
goals.outcome: operators can create safe manual public status incident drafts from internal incidents without leaking private identifiers or raw evidence

goals.title: Monitor readiness: remove Playwright monitors
goals.outcome: Playwright monitor support is removed from product, API, worker dispatch, docs, generated contracts, and upgrade behavior with clear unsupported errors for old configs.

goals.title: Incident readiness: planning state cleanup
goals.outcome: incident planning docs and Maat state clearly distinguish completed lifecycle work from remaining readiness work

goals.title: Monitor readiness: add E2E coverage
goals.outcome: Monitor workflows have repeatable browser or integration coverage for create, test, history, incident, heartbeat, pause, resume, delete, and unsupported-type behavior.

goals.title: Monitor readiness: reconcile planning state
goals.outcome: Maat and repository planning state accurately reflect which monitor work is shipped, deferred, active, or superseded.

tickets.title: Add current Maat instructions to AGENTS
tickets.description: Update AGENTS.md so future agents use the current Maat daily loop, create goals with outcomes, create tickets with descriptions and acceptance criteria, complete work with evidence, and commit finished product changes.
acceptance: AGENTS.md contains the exact current Maat command forms from initialize, including --outcome, --description, --acceptance, claim, comment, complete, validate, and sync commands.

tickets.title: Document Maat setup friction
tickets.description: Capture the friction discovered while initializing and using Maat with Orion, including storage access, setup output, upstream tracking, SQLite cache dirtiness, push boundaries, and ticket state clarity.
acceptance: The friction points are captured in Markdown with concrete symptoms and recommended improvements, and the result is available for future Maat product work.

tickets.title: Split agent availability from monitor health
tickets.description: Implement and document a clearer health model that separates agent process availability, missing agent reports, stale monitor reports, monitor check failures, and threshold-based monitor rollups.
acceptance: Agent and monitor health are computed and presented so one or a few monitor failures do not incorrectly make a live agent look fully down, and the UI explains why a status is degraded or down.

tickets.title: Add report metadata and raw inspection drawers
tickets.description: Preserve useful success and failure context for monitor and agent reports, then expose it in Console drawers from report and history rows.
acceptance: Clicking relevant agent report or monitor history rows opens an inspection drawer showing summarized status, timestamps, metrics, errors, metadata, and raw JSON payload.

tickets.title: Implement manual incident acknowledge and resolve flows
tickets.description: Add backend and Console flows for manually acknowledging and resolving incidents, and handle incidents tied to monitors that are removed from config.
acceptance: Users can acknowledge and resolve incidents from Console, incident events record the manual action, and deleting or unregistering a monitor resolves or resets its active incident path cleanly.

tickets.title: Expose diagnostics endpoint and ingestion latency signals
tickets.description: Add read-only Core diagnostics that track request counts, ingestion latency, report writes, incident reconciliation timing, SQLite busy events, database size, and slow operations.
