tickets.description: Move Core monitor kind alias normalization and config or secret redaction helpers into the validation package with focused unit tests.
acceptance: Normalization and redaction have focused tests, API response redaction behavior is unchanged, and cd apps/core && go test ./internal/service ./internal/api passes.

tickets.title: Move type-specific Core monitor validators
tickets.description: Move per-kind Core monitor config validation for heartbeat, HTTP, TCP, DNS, TLS, UDP, API request, domain expiration, ping, mail, synthetic, and Playwright monitors into the validation package.
acceptance: Each supported kind has table-driven validation coverage, invalid configs still return 400 through API handlers, worker runner parsing remains separate unless explicitly justified, and cd apps/core && go test ./... passes.

tickets.title: Design Core monitor validation package boundary
tickets.description: Define and implement the internal package boundary for Core monitor kind aliases, config validation, redaction, and validation errors without moving lifecycle persistence.
acceptance: A focused internal package exists or a clear package decision is documented in code structure, lifecycle persistence remains in the service, and existing Core monitor tests still pass.

tickets.title: Add Core race and contract gates
tickets.description: Add or document CI gates for go test -race on Core service and worker packages plus generated OpenAPI and SDK drift checks.
acceptance: CI or Make targets run go test -race ./internal/service ./internal/worker, generated contract drift is checked, and the expected commands are documented.

tickets.title: Document Core backend verification commands
tickets.description: Document the required local and CI verification commands for Core cleanup PRs, including tests, race checks, generated contracts, vulncheck, and modernization linting.
acceptance: Docs identify the required commands for Core cleanup work, distinguish required versus optional gates, and match the implemented CI or Make targets.

tickets.title: Add Core govulncheck and modernize lint gates
tickets.description: Add Core backend quality gates for govulncheck and golangci-lint v2.6+ with the modernize linter, using advisory behavior first if current findings require follow-up.
acceptance: CI or Make targets can run govulncheck and golangci-lint for apps/core, modernize findings are fixed or documented as follow-up tickets, and existing Core tests pass.

tickets.title: Modernize Core interface and JSON helper usage
tickets.description: Replace obvious owned non-generated Core interface{} usage with any and introduce or reuse small JSON helpers where it improves readability without changing persisted or response JSON shape.
acceptance: Non-generated Core readability changes are mechanical, generated Swagger files are untouched, JSON response and persistence shapes are unchanged, and cd apps/core && go test ./... passes.

tickets.title: Modernize Core sorting collection and context patterns
tickets.description: Apply safe Go modernizations in Core for simple sort.Slice replacements, slices/maps helpers, min/max usage, and t.Context in eligible tests where readability improves.
acceptance: Modernizations are limited to owned non-generated Core code, no behavior changes are introduced, and cd apps/core && go test ./... passes.

tickets.title: Evaluate Core Go 1.26 upgrade readiness
tickets.description: Evaluate whether apps/core can safely move from Go 1.25.3 to Go 1.26 after tests, CI, generated contracts, Docker builds, and release packaging are proven.
acceptance: A documented readiness note or implementation shows the Go 1.26 decision, all required checks pass if upgraded, and no Go 1.26-only syntax is introduced before CI support is confirmed.

tickets.title: Add Core backend PR coverage checklist
tickets.description: Add a PR checklist or contributor documentation requiring the right backend test level for Core route, service, worker, migration, contract, and public/private boundary changes.
acceptance: Core backend PR guidance exists, missing tests must be justified or ticketed, and the guidance distinguishes unit, service, API, worker, migration, contract, and security coverage.

tickets.title: Add coverage reporting for Core backend packages
tickets.description: Add package-level Core backend coverage reporting that makes gaps visible without using raw line coverage as the only quality signal.
acceptance: CI or local commands report Core package coverage, generated docs are excluded or interpreted correctly, and docs explain how to use coverage reports pragmatically.

tickets.title: Enforce test expectations for new Core backend work
tickets.description: Add lightweight enforcement or review automation so new Core backend routes, service methods, worker runners, and migrations include required tests or create explicit follow-up tickets.
acceptance: New Core backend work has an enforceable checklist, script, or CI-supported review gate, and exceptions require documented follow-up tickets with acceptance criteria.

tickets.title: Repair Console smoke navigation selectors
tickets.description: Update Playwright smoke tests after the Agent to Server product-language migration so primary navigation reaches Servers and continues through Settings.
acceptance: pnpm test:e2e no longer fails on stale Agents selectors and the primary operations smoke test reaches the Settings page.

tickets.title: Add Settings read and save browser coverage
tickets.description: Extend the Console Playwright smoke suite to verify Settings seeded values and a successful lifecycle settings update through the browser.
acceptance: E2E coverage asserts seeded lifecycle values, updates valid values, sees a saved state, and verifies the refreshed field values without relying on implementation details.

tickets.title: Add Settings validation and action browser coverage
tickets.description: Extend Playwright coverage for invalid lifecycle settings, manual rollup, and manual archive flows.
acceptance: E2E coverage confirms invalid settings show a failure state, manual rollup returns a result, manual archive returns a result or expected skipped state, and pnpm test:e2e passes.

tickets.title: Define archive directory policy
tickets.description: Define the Core policy for data lifecycle archive directories, including default safe base behavior and whether external archive directories require explicit configuration.
acceptance: A documented policy is implemented in code or docs before validation changes, and the decision states how relative, absolute, traversal, and empty archive paths behave.

tickets.title: Enforce safe archive path validation
tickets.description: Update Core settings validation and archive path resolution so unsafe archive directories are rejected or normalized according to the chosen policy.
acceptance: Core rejects traversal and unsafe external archive paths, preserves valid defaults, avoids leaking host filesystem details in API errors, and focused settings/archive tests pass.

tickets.title: Expand lifecycle settings security tests
tickets.description: Add Core tests for lifecycle settings security boundaries, including path traversal, absolute paths, empty archive path with archiving enabled, invalid schedules, and excessive retention values.
acceptance: Core tests cover the security boundary cases and fail without the validation policy; go test ./internal/service ./internal/api -run 'Test(DataLifecycle|SettingsService)' passes.

tickets.title: Update browser smoke for Servers navigation
tickets.description: Update apps/console/e2e/console-smoke.spec.ts so primary navigation assertions use Servers instead of stale Agents copy while preserving coverage of the redirected /agents route.
acceptance: Playwright smoke asserts Servers navigation, verifies /agents redirects to /servers, and apps/console pnpm run test:e2e passes.

tickets.title: Stabilize incident lifecycle e2e flow
tickets.description: Verify and fix incident detail browser smoke for report inspection, notification tabs, cover, reopen, acknowledge, and resolve flows.
acceptance: Incident lifecycle Playwright scenario passes and validates the key controls, tabs, and success states against seeded Core data.

tickets.title: Document e2e smoke prerequisites
tickets.description: Document the local prerequisites and troubleshooting notes for running Console Playwright smoke, including loopback server requirements and seeded Core data expectations.
acceptance: Console or development docs explain how to run pnpm run test:e2e, what services it starts, and common failure causes such as stale nav labels or blocked loopback binds.

tickets.title: Stabilize monitor and heartbeat e2e flows
tickets.description: Verify and fix Core HTTP monitor CRUD/test/pause/resume/delete and Core heartbeat create/signal/redaction flows in browser smoke after navigation terminology changes.
acceptance: Monitor and heartbeat Playwright scenarios pass consistently and still assert secret redaction in monitor, incident, and history views.

tickets.title: Add archive confirmation workflow
tickets.description: Add a Console confirmation step before manual archive execution that states cutoff, archive destination, and that old raw reports will move out of the hot Core database.
acceptance: Manual archive cannot run from the Settings page without confirmation, the dialog uses current settings data, and UI tests or e2e coverage verify the confirmation path.

tickets.title: Audit Console Server copy
tickets.description: Audit apps/console visible copy for Agent and Agents references and update product-facing labels to Server or Servers where compatibility does not require agent wording.
acceptance: Console user-visible copy consistently uses Server terminology, compatibility-only agent strings are documented, and apps/console pnpm run build passes.

tickets.title: Record compatibility terminology decision
tickets.description: Add or update an architecture decision note that records Server as product terminology and agent as internal compatibility terminology for routes, generated SDK names, database tables, and binary paths.
acceptance: Decision note exists, names compatibility boundaries, and links to docs or code areas intentionally left as agent.

tickets.title: Update docs for Server terminology boundary
tickets.description: Update README and architecture or deployment docs to explain Server as the product term while retaining orion-agent binary, service, config, and API compatibility names.
acceptance: Docs clearly describe the terminology boundary and do not imply binary, route, or config renames that are not implemented.

tickets.title: Refresh Server screenshots and captions
tickets.description: Audit product screenshots, image names, captions, and README preview text for stale Agents terminology and refresh only the assets or captions needed for public evaluation.
acceptance: README preview and referenced assets no longer expose stale user-facing Agents terminology unless deliberately documenting compatibility.

tickets.title: Improve manual lifecycle result states
tickets.description: Improve Settings manual rollup and archive result rendering so operators see counts, skipped states, timestamps, and safe error messages.
acceptance: Settings displays rollup report count, archive report counts, skipped disabled/no-report states, last-run timestamps, and user-safe errors after actions.

tickets.title: Add production auth config guard
tickets.description: Design and implement a runtime guard or explicit configuration mode that prevents accidental public Core operation with frontend auth disabled while preserving intentional local development behavior.
acceptance: Core has a tested fail-closed or warning-plus-explicit-opt-in behavior for auth-disabled public deployments, and local development remains documented.

tickets.title: Test frontend auth secure defaults
tickets.description: Add Core tests for frontend route behavior under auth-enabled, auth-disabled local, and production-like auth-disabled configurations.
acceptance: Tests prove protected frontend routes require JWT when configured and prove the new public deployment guard behavior.

tickets.title: Audit public route privacy boundaries
tickets.description: Audit public status, heartbeat, health, swagger, and console fallback routes for secret exposure, unintended admin data, and safe error messages.
acceptance: Audit findings are fixed or ticketed, public routes avoid internal-only data exposure, and focused tests cover any changed behavior.

tickets.title: Document required deployment secrets
tickets.description: Update deployment docs and examples to make ORION_ADMIN_USERNAME, ORION_ADMIN_PASSWORD, ORION_JWT_SECRET, and public status subscriber secrets explicit and non-optional for production-like deployments.
acceptance: Docker Compose, README, and deployment docs clearly state required secrets and include safe placeholder guidance without implying defaults are production-safe.

tickets.title: Prevent duplicate lifecycle action execution
tickets.description: Ensure manual archive and rollup actions cannot be double-submitted from the Console and that concurrent pending states are clear.
acceptance: Action buttons are disabled while pending, duplicate clicks do not submit multiple mutations, and browser coverage or component-level verification confirms the pending behavior.

tickets.title: Add demo healthy and failing states
tickets.description: Ensure the first-run example can demonstrate both healthy and failing service states without editing product code or relying on external services.
acceptance: The example includes documented commands to produce healthy and failing monitor states and explains the expected incident or health changes.

tickets.title: Document demo Console verification path
tickets.description: Document the exact Console path for verifying the first-run demo: Server appears, monitor appears, incident opens, report/log evidence is inspectable, and reset works.
acceptance: Example docs include concise expected screens or labels and reset instructions that match current Console terminology.

tickets.title: Automate first-run demo smoke
tickets.description: Add a lightweight script or documented command set that validates the first-run demo enough to catch broken Compose files, config paths, and Core health expectations.
acceptance: A local command validates the demo setup path or docs provide an explicit smoke checklist, and repository smoke includes feasible syntax checks.

tickets.title: Promote Python sleep example
tickets.description: Make examples/python-sleep-compose the canonical first-run demo in README and docs, with a minimal path from Core startup to visible Server and monitor data.
acceptance: A new evaluator can find and run the Python sleep example from README and see the expected Core and Console outcomes.

tickets.title: Emit audit events for settings updates
tickets.description: Record Core audit events when data lifecycle settings are changed through the frontend API, including actor context when available.
acceptance: Updating /v1/settings/data-lifecycle writes an audit event with actor id, action type, and safe metadata; API tests verify the event.

tickets.title: Emit audit events for lifecycle actions
tickets.description: Record Core audit events when manual rollup and archive actions run, including result status and safe counts.
acceptance: Manual rollup and archive endpoints write audit events with actor id when available, action result metadata, and no unsafe filesystem details beyond approved archive path representation.

tickets.title: Improve status page publish workflow
tickets.description: Improve status page authoring and publish validation so missing component mappings, unpublished incidents, visibility state, and public preview gaps are obvious before publishing.
acceptance: Status page editor shows actionable publish blockers and warnings, preview matches public output, and relevant API and Console checks pass.

tickets.title: Add operational empty and error states
tickets.description: Audit operational Console pages for seeded-data assumptions and add useful empty, loading, error, and retry states where missing.
acceptance: Incidents, Servers, Monitors, Alerts, Status Pages, Logs, and Settings handle empty and error states without broken layout or misleading copy.

tickets.title: Complete Core monitor type Console workflows
tickets.description: Audit supported Core monitor types and implement or ticket missing Console create edit test details for each type surfaced by the backend.
acceptance: Console either supports each backend Core monitor type intentionally or documents unsupported types with concrete follow-up tickets, and build plus relevant tests pass.

tickets.title: Improve alert workflow usability
tickets.description: Improve alert rule, destination, dry-run, delivery log, and failed delivery explanation workflows so an operator can configure and debug alerts without reading raw database state.
acceptance: Alert UI exposes clear configuration and failure feedback for supported alert paths, and backend/Console tests cover changed behavior.

tickets.title: Expose lifecycle audit events in Logs
tickets.description: Ensure lifecycle settings and manual maintenance audit events are visible and filterable from the Console Logs surface.
acceptance: Logs can show and filter lifecycle settings update, rollup run, and archive run events; Console or e2e verification covers the visible event types.

tickets.title: Restructure Settings into operator sections
tickets.description: Refactor the Settings page into clear sections for Retention Policy, Archive Storage, Rollups, Manual Maintenance, and Recent Activity without changing API behavior.
acceptance: The Settings page has scannable operator sections, no nested card clutter, and preserves existing read/update/action behavior.

tickets.title: Classify known release warnings
tickets.description: Classify known warnings such as Vite chunk size, sandbox-only loopback restrictions, dirty generated indexes, and Maat push policy as blockers, accepted warnings, or follow-up tickets.
acceptance: Known warnings are documented with clear release impact and follow-up ownership where they are not blockers.

tickets.title: Wire release gates into CI or Make
tickets.description: Add or align Make and CI targets so release gates can be run consistently without relying on remembered manual commands.
acceptance: Make or CI targets cover Core tests, Agent tests, Console lint/build/e2e, generated contracts, Docker Compose config, and Docker image build where feasible.

tickets.title: Define release verification matrix
tickets.description: Document the required release verification matrix for Core, Agent, Console, generated contracts, Docker Compose, Docker image, and browser smoke.
acceptance: Release docs or checklist identify exact commands, owners, expected outputs, and blocker status for each verification gate.

tickets.title: Add PR readiness template
tickets.description: Add a PR readiness template or checklist that requires evidence for tests, generated contracts, docs, deployment impact, security impact, and e2e status before merging into main.
acceptance: New PRs have a concise readiness checklist and release-impact prompts that match the verification matrix.

tickets.title: Add inline lifecycle form validation
tickets.description: Add client-side validation for lifecycle Settings numeric bounds, required archive path, and incompatible archive and rollup options before submit.
acceptance: Invalid lifecycle form states show inline errors, Save settings is blocked or produces a clear field-level message, and server-side validation remains authoritative.

tickets.title: Add direct Core service unit coverage
tickets.description: Add focused service-level tests for Core exported service methods that are currently exercised mostly through API integration tests, prioritizing AgentService, MonitorService, IncidentService, RuntimeDiagnosticsService, and AuthService.
acceptance: Service package tests cover happy path, invalid input, auth or ownership boundary, and persistence side effects for the prioritized exported service methods, and cd apps/core && go test ./internal/service passes.

tickets.title: Add lifecycle recent activity context
tickets.description: Add a Settings recent activity area that summarizes last rollup, last archive, status, and relevant action results in operator language.
acceptance: Settings shows recent lifecycle activity with timestamps, statuses, safe errors, and result counts where available, and existing date formatting remains consistent.

tickets.title: Add Core startup and fallback route coverage
tickets.description: Add tests or explicit contract checks for Core startup wiring, migrated database opening, Swagger route availability, and Console fallback behavior that are not covered by current backend tests.
acceptance: Startup and fallback coverage is either tested in Core or intentionally deferred with documented rationale, and cd apps/core && go test ./... passes.

tickets.title: Update lifecycle architecture docs
tickets.description: Update persistence and lifecycle architecture documentation for archive path policy, audit events, and manual action behavior after implementation.
acceptance: docs/architecture/persistence-and-lifecycle.md reflects implemented Settings behavior and does not describe stale lifecycle behavior.

tickets.title: Add Core route coverage drift check
tickets.description: Add a lightweight check that compares registered Core routes against the backend coverage inventory or route-test map so new routes cannot silently ship without an explicit coverage status.
acceptance: A deterministic route inventory or test guard fails when setupRoutes gains an unmapped route, and the Core verification command documented for backend work includes the check.

tickets.title: Reconcile Settings readiness Maat state
tickets.description: Review prior Settings audit and browser-smoke tickets, add comments or completions as appropriate, and remove ambiguity about what remains open.
acceptance: Relevant Maat tickets have factual comments or completion evidence tying them to the new Settings readiness goals, and no title-only or duplicate Settings tickets remain unexplained.

tickets.title: Add Settings PR closeout checklist
tickets.description: Add or update a Settings readiness checklist that each goal PR can use for tests, generated files, docs, Maat comments, and PR evidence.
acceptance: A concise checklist exists in repo docs or Maat comments and covers tests, generated contract handling, docs updates, and PR evidence for Settings readiness work.

tickets.title: Stabilize status page E2E harness
tickets.description: Make the Console Playwright harness safe to run for status page workflows by avoiding fixed-port conflicts or documenting and enforcing a clean isolated run path.
acceptance: pnpm --filter @sunday-studio/orion-console test:e2e can start its own Core and Console or fails with a clear documented prerequisite, and status page E2E specs can run reliably in CI/local smoke.

tickets.title: Add status page create publish browser flow
tickets.description: Add Playwright coverage that creates a status page, adds a section, adds a public component, maps a monitor, publishes the page, opens the public URL, and verifies the public component status.
acceptance: A browser E2E test covers the happy path from authenticated Console creation through unauthenticated public status page rendering and passes locally with seeded Core data.

tickets.title: Repair incident smoke navigation
tickets.description: Update Console Playwright smoke navigation selectors and assertions so the suite follows current Servers terminology and no longer fails before incident tests run.
acceptance: pnpm run test:e2e reaches and runs the incident lifecycle test instead of failing on a stale Agents navigation selector.

tickets.title: Add public incident lifecycle browser flow
tickets.description: Add Playwright coverage for drafting, publishing, updating, resolving, and viewing a public status page incident and its public history.
acceptance: E2E verifies public incident lifecycle actions from Console and checks unauthenticated incident detail/history routes show only published public copy.

tickets.title: Stabilize incident lifecycle browser flow
tickets.description: Keep and harden the incident detail e2e flow for evidence inspection, notifications, monitor reports, cover, reopen, acknowledge, and resolve actions against seeded Core data.
acceptance: Focused incident Playwright test passes and remains part of the full browser smoke suite with stable selectors and seeded data assumptions documented in the test.

tickets.title: Add public status privacy leak E2E assertions
tickets.description: Add browser-level assertions that public status page, public incident, feed, badge, and history surfaces do not expose internal monitor IDs, server IDs, tokens, raw payloads, or private incident details.
acceptance: Status page E2E includes explicit negative assertions for internal identifiers and secret-like values across the public surfaces touched by the flow.

tickets.title: Add incident list detail navigation regression
tickets.description: Add browser coverage that enters incident detail from the incident list and returns without losing URL-backed filters or pagination state.
acceptance: Playwright verifies incident list filters survive detail navigation and the test is included in the incident e2e smoke path.

tickets.title: Sanitize public theme settings at projection time
tickets.description: Apply public-safe theme_settings sanitization when building public status page DTOs so legacy or direct database rows cannot expose unsupported keys.
acceptance: Public JSON and HTML projections include only supported theme setting keys even when the database row contains unsupported theme_settings, with regression tests.

tickets.title: Expand public DTO privacy regression tests
tickets.description: Add focused Core tests that public status page JSON, incident detail, history, subscriber response, and preview projections omit internal fields and private records.
acceptance: Core tests fail if public DTOs expose monitor IDs, server IDs, raw payloads, private incident visibility, unpublished updates, destination hashes, ciphertext, or token hashes.

tickets.title: Persist incident lifecycle actor and note metadata
tickets.description: Extend Core incident lifecycle actions and persistence so acknowledge, resolve, cover, and reopen preserve actor identity, timestamps, and human notes without relying on latest_event text for product logic.
acceptance: Core migrations, service methods, API responses, and tests expose lifecycle actor and note metadata for all manual incident actions.

tickets.title: Harden public HTML feed badge history tests
tickets.description: Add or tighten tests for public HTML, Atom feed, SVG badges, component uptime, component history, and incident history privacy and cache behavior.
acceptance: Focused Core tests cover public rendering surfaces for no private data leakage, correct content type/cache headers where applicable, and draft/unpublished record suppression.

tickets.title: Return incident allowed action state
tickets.description: Add Core incident detail action availability fields so Console can render lifecycle actions from server-backed rules rather than duplicating status logic.
acceptance: GET /v1/incidents/{id} returns allowed action booleans covered by API tests and Console uses them for action visibility.

tickets.title: Re-audit custom domain status routing isolation
tickets.description: Review and extend custom-domain status page routing tests so host-based public pages cannot expose another page by slug or fall through into authenticated Console routes incorrectly.
acceptance: Tests cover custom-domain host normalization, slug isolation on custom hosts, draft/unlisted/public routing behavior, and safe fallback behavior.

tickets.title: Render lifecycle audit details in Console
tickets.description: Update incident detail to show action actor, note, acknowledged/resolved/reopened metadata, and lifecycle history without cluttering primary evidence.
acceptance: Console incident detail displays structured lifecycle metadata after manual actions and browser coverage verifies the visible state.

tickets.title: Add status page subscriber admin list API
tickets.description: Add authenticated Core admin routes to list public status page subscribers with masked destination, state, timestamps, component preferences, and delivery summary only.
acceptance: GET /v1/status-pages/:id/subscribers returns masked subscriber records, excludes raw destinations, hashes, ciphertext, token hashes, and token versions, and is covered by API tests and OpenAPI/SDK generation if route contracts change.

tickets.title: Generalize monitor report payload redaction
tickets.description: Extend Core monitor report response redaction beyond heartbeat payloads so sensitive keys are redacted for all monitor report payloads before incident evidence or report drawers receive them.
acceptance: API response tests prove token password secret api key authorization and private key values are redacted for non-heartbeat monitor report payloads.

tickets.title: Add status page subscriber disable API
tickets.description: Add authenticated Core admin route to disable a public status page subscriber for abuse, support, or compliance reasons without deleting the row.
acceptance: POST /v1/status-pages/:id/subscribers/:subscriber_id/disable marks the subscriber disabled, suppresses future fan-out, records an audit event, and has API tests for auth, page scoping, and redaction.

tickets.title: Add subscriber anonymize and hard-delete APIs
tickets.description: Add authenticated Core admin routes to anonymize or hard-delete public status page subscribers according to the documented privacy lifecycle.
acceptance: Anonymize clears destination ciphertext and token hashes while preserving safe audit facts; hard-delete removes dependent preferences and delivery rows before subscriber deletion; both routes are authenticated, page-scoped, audited, tested, and documented in OpenAPI/SDK if contracts change.

tickets.title: Add Console subscriber management tab
tickets.description: Add a subscribers tab to the Console status page editor for masked subscriber inspection, state filtering, component preference visibility, delivery summary, disable, anonymize, and hard-delete actions.
acceptance: Console exposes subscriber management without raw destinations or token data, handles empty/error/loading states, and builds successfully against generated SDK types.

tickets.title: Verify incident evidence redaction in Console
tickets.description: Add Console browser coverage that opens incident evidence and raw report drawers containing seeded sensitive values and verifies secrets are not visible.
acceptance: Playwright incident evidence test fails if seeded sensitive payload values appear in incident detail or report inspection UI.

tickets.title: Implement subscriber retention cleanup service
tickets.description: Add Core service logic to find expired pending, unsubscribed, and bounced public status page subscribers that are eligible for anonymization or deletion under configured/default retention windows.
acceptance: Retention cleanup logic is isolated in a service with tests for pending expiry, unsubscribe retention, bounced retention, disabled exclusion, and safe dependency cleanup.

tickets.title: Document incident payload privacy boundary
tickets.description: Update incident or report-inspection documentation to state which payload fields are redacted, which authenticated users can see report evidence, and what remains intentionally visible.
acceptance: Docs describe incident evidence privacy behavior and tests referenced by the implementation verify the boundary.

tickets.title: Schedule subscriber privacy retention job
tickets.description: Wire subscriber retention cleanup into Core's data lifecycle scheduler or an equivalent scheduled maintenance path without coupling it to public request handling.
acceptance: Retention cleanup runs on a bounded schedule, is configurable or uses documented defaults, logs safe summaries, and has tests or documented smoke evidence.

tickets.title: Add incident next action panel
tickets.description: Design and implement an incident detail next-action panel that surfaces the most useful operator actions based on notification status, lifecycle state, monitor type, recurring failures, and status-page availability.
acceptance: Incident detail shows context-aware next actions and avoids generic explanatory copy when no action is available.

tickets.title: Audit subscriber privacy lifecycle mutations
tickets.description: Record audit events for subscriber disable, anonymize, hard-delete, pending purge, unsubscribe retention expiry, and bounced retention expiry without storing raw destination or token data.
acceptance: Audit records identify action, actor/job, status page id, subscriber id, previous state, reason, retention basis, and deletion mode while excluding raw destination values, hashes, ciphertext, token hashes, provider ids, and unsafe mail errors.

tickets.title: Link incident failures to monitor tuning
tickets.description: Add incident detail affordances that take operators directly to relevant monitor configuration for confirmation period, recovery period, maintenance, and type-specific settings when those are likely fixes.
acceptance: Incident detail exposes monitor tuning links for active monitor incidents and browser coverage verifies the links land on the monitor configuration surface.

tickets.title: Document subscriber privacy retention operations
tickets.description: Update status page architecture and deployment/operator docs with implemented subscriber retention windows, backup erasure caveats, and operational verification commands.
acceptance: Docs match implemented retention behavior, include exact default windows and backup caveats, and explain how operators can verify cleanup without exposing subscriber secrets.

tickets.title: Expose notification recovery actions from incidents
tickets.description: Surface failed notification context and useful recovery actions from incident detail, such as opening alert rules, testing destinations, or retrying where supported.
acceptance: Incident detail gives a concrete fix path for failed incident notifications and includes tests for the failed-delivery state.

tickets.title: Define first-release monitor scope
tickets.description: Define the first-release monitor type list as HTTP status HTTP keyword heartbeat TCP DNS TLS and API request; mark every other monitor type as deferred or removed.
acceptance: Docs and product-facing copy name supported first-release monitor types and explicitly mark deferred or removed monitor types without implying Console support.

tickets.title: Restructure status page editor into tabs
tickets.description: Refactor the Console status page editor into staged tabs for basics, components, incidents, subscribers, settings, and preview while preserving existing functionality.
acceptance: The editor is easier to navigate, does not regress existing create/edit/publish flows, handles responsive layout cleanly, and passes Console build plus relevant browser smoke coverage.

tickets.title: Align Console monitor copy with supported scope
tickets.description: Audit Console monitor labels empty states filters and dialogs so unsupported monitor types are not presented as creatable first-release workflows.
acceptance: Console labels and options reflect supported first-release monitor scope while still allowing existing historical rows to be viewed safely.

tickets.title: Update monitor roadmap and docs scope
tickets.description: Update monitor roadmap docs README and deployment docs so backend catalog history does not read as current product promise where Console support is absent.
acceptance: Monitor docs distinguish shipped backend runners from supported product workflows and pass markdown review without stale Playwright-first-release claims.

tickets.title: Open Console monitor creation PR
tickets.description: Coordinate Console creation workflow work and open a PR into main after supported first-release monitor forms are complete.
acceptance: A PR into main exists with Console form changes generated SDK or OpenAPI updates if required and verification notes.

tickets.title: Add TCP DNS TLS Core monitor forms
tickets.description: Add type-specific Console create edit and test form support for TCP DNS and TLS Core monitors.
acceptance: Users can create edit test pause resume and delete TCP DNS and TLS Core monitors from Console without raw JSON entry.

tickets.title: Surface Core validation errors in monitor dialog
tickets.description: Improve Core monitor dialog error handling so backend validation errors are displayed directly and mapped to the current type where practical.
acceptance: Invalid monitor configs return actionable dialog feedback and covered validation states have frontend or integration tests.

tickets.title: Add type-specific monitor result summaries
tickets.description: Replace generic latest-result fields with type-aware summaries for HTTP TCP DNS TLS heartbeat and API request monitors.
acceptance: Monitor detail shows the most relevant status latency target and failure fields for each first-release monitor type without requiring raw payload inspection.

tickets.title: Open scope honesty PR
tickets.description: Coordinate the scope honesty goal and open a PR into main after docs and Console copy are complete.
acceptance: A PR into main exists with scope docs and copy changes, verification notes, and linked Maat evidence.

tickets.title: Add API request Core monitor form
tickets.description: Add Console create edit and test form support for API request monitors including method URL expected status headers body and safe secret handling.
acceptance: Users can configure and test API request monitors from Console and secret-bearing fields remain write-only or redacted in responses and UI.

tickets.title: Open monitor detail usefulness PR
tickets.description: Coordinate monitor detail usefulness work and open a PR into main after summaries explanations and layout updates are complete.
acceptance: A PR into main exists with monitor detail UX improvements and verification evidence.

tickets.title: Add Core worker diagnostics Console panel
tickets.description: Add a Console surface for /v1/diagnostics/core-worker showing worker count online stale status and latest heartbeat state.
acceptance: Console exposes Core worker diagnostics and handles unknown healthy degraded and stale worker states.

tickets.title: Warn when Core monitor worker is absent
tickets.description: Show a visible warning on Core monitor list or detail views when the worker is missing or stale and Core-managed monitors cannot run reliably.
acceptance: Core-owned monitor rows or detail pages clearly warn when worker diagnostics indicate missing or stale execution capacity.

tickets.title: Compact monitor schedule and incident context
tickets.description: Rework monitor detail header or summary panels to show current health owner source last checked next run last success and active incident context together.
acceptance: A user can understand monitor state schedule and incident impact from the first screen of monitor detail on desktop and mobile.

tickets.title: Add monitor failure explanation mapping
tickets.description: Create a small failure explanation layer for common failure_stage error and status payloads across supported monitor types.
acceptance: Monitor detail and test-now feedback provide concise actionable explanations for common failures while raw report inspection remains available.

tickets.title: Add monitor target policy E2E checks
tickets.description: Add tests covering blocked localhost blocked metadata IP blocked redirect to private target and allowed public target behavior for Core monitors.
acceptance: Automated tests prove Core monitor create update test-now and worker execution enforce target policy boundaries.

tickets.title: Improve blocked target UI feedback
tickets.description: Show clear Console feedback when Core rejects private localhost metadata or otherwise blocked monitor targets.
acceptance: Users see actionable blocked-target errors without leaking secret URL query or credential details.

tickets.title: Open worker health PR
tickets.description: Coordinate worker health product work and open a PR into main after diagnostics UI and warnings are complete.
acceptance: A PR into main exists with worker health visibility changes and verification evidence.

tickets.title: Keep API and worker health separated in UI
tickets.description: Ensure Console health displays do not collapse Core API health and Core monitor worker health into one ambiguous state.
acceptance: API health and worker health are labeled separately and tests or screenshots cover the distinction.

tickets.title: Require safe auth posture for exposed deployments
tickets.description: Add config docs and startup or deployment guardrails that make unauthenticated frontend admin APIs unsafe for exposed deployments.
acceptance: Production or exposed deployment docs and guard behavior clearly require ORION_ADMIN_USERNAME ORION_ADMIN_PASSWORD and ORION_JWT_SECRET for monitor admin APIs.

tickets.title: Open monitor security PR
tickets.description: Coordinate monitor security hardening work and open a PR into main after tests UI feedback and docs are complete.
acceptance: A PR into main exists with monitor security hardening and verification evidence.

tickets.title: Add monitor create test history E2E
tickets.description: Add browser or integration coverage for Core monitor create create-and-test history row report inspection pause resume and delete workflows.
acceptance: Automated e2e coverage exercises the happy path for supported Core monitor workflow actions.

tickets.title: Remove Playwright worker runner
tickets.description: Remove Playwright monitor execution from worker dispatch and delete or quarantine Playwright runner code and tests.
acceptance: The worker no longer dispatches Playwright checks and Go tests prove Playwright monitor execution is unsupported.

tickets.title: Remove Playwright Console and docs references
tickets.description: Remove Playwright monitor filters creation affordances deployment docs and roadmap references or mark old references as removed historical context.
acceptance: Console and docs no longer present Playwright monitors as supported and historical references explain removal where needed.

tickets.title: Remove Playwright API support
tickets.description: Remove playwright and playwright_transaction from supported Core monitor kind validation create update test-now OpenAPI and generated SDK behavior.
acceptance: Creating updating or testing a Playwright monitor returns a clear unsupported error and generated contracts no longer advertise Playwright monitor support.

tickets.title: Handle existing Playwright monitor configs
tickets.description: Define and implement safe behavior for existing Playwright monitor configs after support is removed.
acceptance: Existing Playwright configs cannot run silently and surface a clear unsupported state without data loss or worker crashes.

tickets.title: Open Playwright removal PR
tickets.description: Coordinate Playwright removal work and open a PR into main after code docs contracts and migration behavior are complete.
acceptance: A PR into main exists that removes Playwright monitor support with tests generated artifacts and Maat evidence.

tickets.title: Add monitor incident E2E coverage
tickets.description: Add e2e coverage proving failing Core monitor reports create or update incidents and recovery resolves or updates incident state according to configured rules.
acceptance: Automated tests cover monitor-driven incident creation and recovery behavior from a user-visible workflow or API-backed browser flow.

tickets.title: Close or supersede stale monitor tickets
tickets.description: Complete duplicate or already-shipped monitor tickets with evidence and comment on deferred or superseded tickets with the new target goal.
acceptance: Maat no longer shows obviously completed monitor runner or workflow tickets as active without an explanatory status note.

tickets.title: Create public incident draft from internal incident
tickets.description: Add a Core and Console flow that lets an operator start a status-page public incident draft from an internal incident using existing component suggestions.
acceptance: From incident detail an operator can choose a status page, review suggested public components, and create a draft public incident linked to the internal incident.

tickets.title: Audit active monitor tickets against code
tickets.description: Compare active monitor Maat tickets to current code docs and milestones to identify shipped deferred duplicate and superseded work.
acceptance: A written audit maps active monitor tickets into shipped deferred duplicate superseded or still-needed categories with evidence links.

tickets.title: Open monitor E2E PR
tickets.description: Coordinate monitor E2E coverage work and open a PR into main after workflow coverage is stable.
acceptance: A PR into main exists with monitor E2E coverage docs and verification notes.

tickets.title: Add heartbeat E2E coverage
tickets.description: Add e2e coverage for heartbeat monitor creation success signal failure signal setup panel and missed heartbeat behavior where feasible.
acceptance: Heartbeat workflows have repeatable automated verification with clear prerequisites for any time-dependent missed-signal case.

tickets.title: Open planning reconciliation PR
tickets.description: Coordinate planning reconciliation work and open a PR into main after Maat and docs are aligned.
acceptance: A PR into main exists with planning documentation updates and Maat validation evidence.

tickets.title: Update monitor milestone docs after reconciliation
tickets.description: Update monitor milestone and planning docs so they reflect the post-reconciliation state and Playwright removal decision.
acceptance: Monitor planning docs match current Maat state and repository behavior after stale ticket cleanup.

tickets.title: Add status page publish validation panel
tickets.description: Expose publish validation errors and warnings in Console before publishing, with clear affected component or incident context.
acceptance: Operators can inspect validation output before publish, publish remains blocked on server-side errors, warnings are visible, and publish error handling no longer relies on a generic failure sentence.

tickets.title: Generate safe public draft copy
tickets.description: Build safe public draft title impact summary and initial update defaults using public component names and approved templates only, never internal event text or raw reports.
acceptance: Tests prove public draft creation omits internal IDs hostnames raw payload text and sensitive values while producing useful editable public copy.

tickets.title: Add status page deletion and removal flows
tickets.description: Add safe delete/remove flows for status pages, sections, components, mappings, public incidents, and public incident updates where the backend supports or should support them.
acceptance: Users can remove draft or obsolete status-page objects with confirmations; backend routes and tests exist where missing; generated contracts and SDK are updated when APIs change.

tickets.title: Add public draft workflow browser coverage
tickets.description: Add Playwright coverage for creating a public status incident draft from an internal incident and verifying it remains draft until explicitly published.
acceptance: Browser test covers internal incident to public draft creation and proves the public status route does not expose the draft before publication.

tickets.title: Add public URL feed and badge controls
tickets.description: Add explicit Console controls to open and copy the public status URL, Atom feed URL, page badge URL, and component badge URLs.
acceptance: Status page Console includes clear copy/open controls for public URL, feed, page badge, and component badges, with disabled states for unpublished pages and tests/build verification.

tickets.title: Refresh incident IA documentation
tickets.description: Update incident information architecture and status page incident docs so they no longer describe completed lifecycle work as missing and accurately record remaining readiness work.
acceptance: Docs distinguish implemented incident lifecycle evidence analytics and coverage behavior from remaining metadata next-action and public draft workflow gaps.

tickets.title: Decide status page first-release cardinality
tickets.description: Make an explicit product decision whether the first status page release supports one page or multiple pages and record the rationale against current implementation behavior.
acceptance: A status page architecture decision states singleton or multiple-page release behavior, why it was chosen, and what implementation or docs changes are required.

tickets.title: Reconcile stale incident Maat phase rows
tickets.description: Review active-looking legacy incident phase and component tickets against completion events, then comment or complete stale rows so remaining Maat state reflects real work.
acceptance: Maat records identify which legacy incident rows are stale complete versus still actionable, with comments or completion evidence added as appropriate.

tickets.title: Align Console with status page cardinality
tickets.description: Update Console status page create/list behavior to match the chosen first-release cardinality decision.
acceptance: If singleton is chosen, Console prevents accidental second-page creation; if multiple pages are chosen, Console copy and docs stop implying singleton behavior; build and browser smoke pass.

tickets.title: Close incident readiness PR checklist
tickets.description: Create or update the incident readiness closeout checklist so each goal worker records branch, PR, tests, generated files, and Maat evidence before handoff.
acceptance: A documented checklist exists and each incident readiness worker can use it to submit a PR into main with clear verification evidence.

tickets.title: Reconcile status page architecture docs
tickets.description: Update status page architecture and decision docs so planned, shipped, deferred, and first-release behavior are not contradictory.
acceptance: Docs accurately identify shipped public HTML/JSON/history/feed/badge/subscriber/custom-domain behavior, deferred admin subscriber lifecycle work, and the chosen first-release cardinality.

tickets.title: Reconcile status page Maat state
tickets.description: Audit old status page goals and tickets against actual shipped behavior and record which are complete, superseded, or still active.
acceptance: Maat comments or completions clarify stale active status-page tickets without falsely completing work that remains undone, and remaining active work points to current goal streams.

tickets.title: Strengthen status page demo walkthrough
tickets.description: Improve seeded status page demo data and docs so evaluators can immediately see public status, active outage, maintenance, resolved incident history, feed, badge, and subscriber states.
acceptance: Demo seed docs include a concise verification path with exact URLs and expected visible states, and seeded data exercises the full public status page feature without exposing private internals.

tickets.title: Show uptime context in Console status preview
tickets.description: Add public uptime summary or history context to the Console status page preview so operators see what visitors will understand before publishing.
acceptance: Preview shows component uptime/no-data state using public-safe data, handles empty rollups, and passes Console build plus relevant API tests if new endpoints or DTO fields are needed.

tickets.title: Resolve server-mapped component public health
tickets.description: Make public status page components mapped to Servers produce useful public health instead of defaulting to unknown when safe source data exists.
acceptance: Server/agent mappings compute public status from safe health fields, tests cover up/degraded/down/maintenance/unknown behavior, and public DTOs still do not expose server internals.

tickets.title: Document public status page IA and visual design references
tickets.description: Break down the supplied public status page screenshots into information architecture, interaction model, content hierarchy, visual design patterns, reusable components, and implementation implications for Orion public status pages.
acceptance: A repo doc captures the IA-level and design-level analysis of the supplied references, identifies Orion-specific recommendations, and links the work to the status page usefulness roadmap.

tickets.title: Add public publish diff context
tickets.description: Show operators what public-facing fields, component states, incidents, updates, feed entries, and subscription notifications would change before publishing a status page or public incident update.
acceptance: Console provides a public-safe change summary before publish actions, distinguishes page/component/incident/update changes, and does not expose internal IDs or raw operational data.

events.summary: Claimed by codex-m6-tls-monitor-prep. Inspected current Core worker runner with parent TCP edits present in apps/core/internal/worker/app.go. documents.title: Ticket: Verify v1 document-drive acceptance workflow
documents.content:
# Ticket: Verify v1 document-drive acceptance workflow

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260526-215050-9b18 |
| Project | driver |
| Goal | G-20260526-214932-bfdb |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-26T21:50:50+02:00 |

## Description

Prove the product acceptance path from docs/product-plan.md using real or smoke-quality fixtures: drop or upload supported documents, process them, search source and main-language text, inspect document detail, organize with tags/collections, and retry failures without mutating originals.

## Acceptance

- A Dutch scanned PDF can be moved into the configured document directory and found by a Dutch query.
- The same document can be found through the selected main language after translation.
- A DOCX keeps its base format and can be searched, downloaded, and inspected through extracted text.
- OCR, translation, or embedding failure leaves the document visible with retry/status information.
- Evidence names the exact command, fixture, commit, or manual smoke used.

Keep apps/api as the backend owner for configuration, routing, OpenAPI, auth, documents, TUS uploads, search, settings, tags, collections, users/direct grants, ingestion file reports, and job lease/complete/fail routes.

## Acceptance

- Route or response changes update apps/api/openapi.json and generated SDK when the frontend contract changes.
- Protected API responses expose stable IDs and never filesystem paths.
- Relevant unit, integration, and contract tests are added or updated for behavior changes.
- make test-fast or a narrower justified command passes and is recorded as evidence.

documents.title: Ticket: Resolve processing status vocabulary and job timelines
documents.content:
# Ticket: Resolve processing status vocabulary and job timelines

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260526-215134-e395 |
| Project | driver |
| Goal | G-20260526-214940-f930 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-26T21:51:34+02:00 |

## Description

Align the documented processing states with API, SDK, worker, and UI behavior so agents and users can rely on one status model and useful processing history.

## Acceptance

- Document status values are intentionally chosen and reflected consistently in OpenAPI, SDK types, API responses, worker updates, and UI labels.
- Job complete/fail updates preserve enough step/event history for document detail and troubleshooting.
- Any migration or compatibility concern is documented before changing persisted values.
- Contract tests cover the chosen status values and event visibility.

documents.title: Ticket: Maintain watcher registration and document lifecycle
documents.content:
# Ticket: Maintain watcher registration and document lifecycle

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260526-215214-418c |
| Project | driver |
| Goal | G-20260526-214948-2ed0 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-26T21:52:14+02:00 |

## Description

Keep the worker reconciler and orchestrator responsible for scanning the configured document directory, waiting for stable writes, hashing files, registering stable files with the API, handling duplicates/changed paths/deletes, and enqueueing index jobs while TUS remains only an upload path.

## Acceptance

- Partial writes do not enqueue jobs until size and mtime are stable.
- Duplicate checksums, same-path changes, deleted files, unreadable files, and symlink/path safety are tested or intentionally documented.
- Generated artifacts are written under app data and never inside the document directory.
- Worker-to-API registration routes remain protected by worker/admin auth.

documents.title: Ticket: Complete extraction OCR translation embedding pipeline
documents.content:
# Ticket: Complete extraction OCR translation embedding pipeline

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260526-215259-801c |
| Project | driver |
| Goal | G-20260526-214948-2ed0 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-26T21:52:59+02:00 |

## Description

Keep the worker processing pipeline able to extract text from supported formats, OCR scanned/text-poor PDFs with configured languages, chunk by page then length, detect language, translate chunks outside DRIVE_MAIN_LANGUAGE, embed original and translated chunks, and return records for full-text and vector indexing.

## Acceptance

- TXT, RTF, DOCX, ODT, native PDF, and scanned/text-poor PDF behavior is covered by focused or heavy tests as appropriate.
- Translation or embedding provider failure records partial step status and does not hide the document or block original-language search.
- Artifacts include enough tool/version data to support future reprocessing decisions.
- Heavy proof covers real OCR fixtures, pgvector ranking, DOCX, and scanned PDF smoke before this is marked complete.

# Catalog Pattern: Background Refresh

| Field | Value |

| Pattern ID | CP-20260527-background-refresh |
| Project | maat |
| Slug | background-refresh |
| Title | Background refresh |
| Category | background refresh |
| Tags | #tui #refresh #state |

## Problem

Long-running terminal sessions can become misleading when the screen does not reveal stale or refreshed state.

## Observed In

- btop
- gh-dash

## Maat Relevance

Maat should make reloads, storage freshness, and refresh failures clear while preserving navigation context.

## Implementation Notes

Keep refresh state visible, recoverable, and safe for concurrent Markdown updates.

## Related Goals

- G-20260526-220308-c0d2

## Related Tickets

- T-20260526-220336-a45d

Terminal apps become slow when movement, inspection, filtering, and escape paths are inconsistent.

Favor arrow keys and vim-style movement, enter for inspect, slash for search, and backspace or escape for return paths.

documents.title: Event: ticket.commented
documents.content:
# Event: ticket.commented

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Event ID | E-20260529-142406-casprine-2757 |
| Time | 2026-05-29T14:24:06+02:00 |
| Actor | casprine |
| Project | maat |
| Type | ticket.commented |
| Object | T-20260529-141749-4385 |

## Summary

Reviewer synthesis: core change is storage resolution, not project inference. loadStoreAndRest must stop falling through to cwd when config is missing, empty, malformed, or unreadable. Keep cwd-based linked-project inference only after storage is already resolved. Acceptance should cover goal create, ticket create, search, and index rebuild without configured storage failing before dot-maat is created; configured storage from arbitrary cwd; and explicit storage plus project inference.

documents.title: Event: ticket.commented
documents.content:
# Event: ticket.commented

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Event ID | E-20260529-142406-casprine-ca64 |
| Time | 2026-05-29T14:24:06+02:00 |
| Actor | casprine |
| Project | maat |
| Type | ticket.commented |
| Object | T-20260529-141759-1cd9 |

## Summary

Reviewer synthesis: setup ergonomics also includes setup prompt defaults and setup rules, not only initialize and project link. Missing config from a Git source repo must not create projects, setup.md, or dot-maat in cwd. Decide whether setup may default to cwd at all; if it can, require explicit confirmation that the current directory is intended storage.

documents.title: Event: ticket.commented
documents.content:
# Event: ticket.commented

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Event ID | E-20260529-142407-casprine-27fc |
| Time | 2026-05-29T14:24:07+02:00 |
| Actor | casprine |
| Project | maat |
| Type | ticket.commented |
| Object | T-20260529-141807-e579 |

## Summary

Reviewer synthesis: one representative command is under-scoped. Cover every index-writing route or at least the highest-risk paths: search with missing config, search with configured storage from source cwd, index rebuild with missing config, sync without status, project link, initialize, and one write mutation. Include human, JSON, and agent-use error paths where practical, and assert both source cwd has no dot-maat and storage dot-maat exists when indexing is expected.

documents.title: Event: ticket.commented
documents.content:
# Event: ticket.commented

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Event ID | E-20260529-142422-casprine-ebf5 |
| Time | 2026-05-29T14:24:22+02:00 |
| Actor | casprine |
| Project | maat |
| Type | ticket.commented |
| Object | T-20260529-141820-ecec |

## Summary

Reviewer synthesis: docs need to say dot-maat belongs under configured storage, not product repos during normal use. Update storage-model wording, macOS app architecture command examples that omit explicit storage, README/install/search-index cache descriptions to say cache files are not committed, setup docs to state bare commands use configured storage after setup, and architecture wording that overstates SQLite as general fast reads.

documents.title: Event: ticket.commented
documents.content:
# Event: ticket.commented

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Event ID | E-20260529-142423-casprine-5c59 |
| Time | 2026-05-29T14:24:23+02:00 |
| Actor | casprine |
| Project | maat |
| Type | ticket.commented |
| Object | T-20260529-141814-2980 |

## Summary

Reviewer synthesis: lower-level index guards cannot be the policy boundary because WriteIndex and RebuildSQLiteIndex only receive paths and cannot know whether a path came from cwd fallback. Make command-level resolved storage the boundary. Lower-level APIs can still reject empty or non-absolute stores as invariant checks, and the ticket should explicitly decide whether custom SQLite Path is test-only or supported public behavior.

documents.title: Ticket: Define app and CLI update behavior
documents.content:
# Ticket: Define app and CLI update behavior

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260527-122710-bc86 |
| Project | maat |
| Goal | G-20260527-122633-0e69 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-27T12:27:10+02:00 |

## Description

Implement the update model for a Tauri app that ships with a bundled CLI while still allowing the app to know and verify the CLI it is using.

## Acceptance

- App updates can replace the bundled CLI and refresh the app-private installed CLI.
- The app records and displays the active CLI path and version.
- Advanced CLI self-update behavior through maat update --json is either implemented or explicitly deferred.

documents.title: Ticket: Add optional terminal CLI installation
documents.content:
# Ticket: Add optional terminal CLI installation

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260527-122710-fcaa |
| Project | maat |
| Goal | G-20260527-122622-7317 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-27T12:27:10+02:00 |

## Description

Offer a setup or settings action that installs the same maat CLI for terminal use without making shell profile changes mandatory for the desktop app.

## Acceptance

- The desktop app remains usable with only the app-private CLI installed.
- A user can explicitly install or link maat into a PATH directory for terminal use.
- The UI explains the app-private CLI path and terminal CLI path as separate install targets.

documents.title: Ticket: Build read-only desktop dashboard views
documents.content:
# Ticket: Build read-only desktop dashboard views

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260527-122710-fdb4 |
| Project | maat |
| Goal | G-20260527-122633-0e69 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-27T12:27:10+02:00 |

## Description

Implement the first native desktop views for project status, project list, project details, tickets, catalog objects, and search using existing maat CLI JSON commands.

## Acceptance

- The dashboard loads maat status --json and maat projects --json through the command runner.
- Project and ticket detail views use maat project show, ticket list, and ticket show JSON output.
- Catalog and search views use maat catalog list/show and maat search JSON output where available.

documents.title: Ticket: Remove cwd fallback from normal storage resolution
documents.content:
# Ticket: Remove cwd fallback from normal storage resolution

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260529-141749-4385 |
| Project | maat |
| Goal | G-20260529-141731-daf6 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-29T14:17:49+02:00 |

## Description

Change storage resolution so normal commands use explicit --storage or configured storage and otherwise fail instead of treating the current working directory as storage.

## Acceptance

- Commands run from a source repo without --storage and without configured storage return a clear setup error.
- No command in this path creates ./ .maat or ./ .maat/index.sqlite in the source repo.
- Existing configured-storage use still works without passing --storage.

documents.title: Ticket: Preserve setup and initialize ergonomics
documents.content:
# Ticket: Preserve setup and initialize ergonomics

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260529-141759-1cd9 |
| Project | maat |
| Goal | G-20260529-141731-daf6 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-29T14:17:59+02:00 |

## Description

Keep first-run setup usable while making initialize and project link refuse ambiguous storage resolution from source repositories.

## Acceptance

- maat setup can still prompt for or accept an absolute Git-backed storage repo.
- maat initialize and maat project link require configured storage or explicit --storage before writing.
- Error text tells the user to run maat setup --storage /path/to/storage when storage is missing.

documents.title: Ticket: Add regression tests for source repo cache creation
documents.content:
# Ticket: Add regression tests for source repo cache creation

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260529-141807-e579 |
| Project | maat |
| Goal | G-20260529-141731-daf6 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-29T14:18:07+02:00 |

## Description

Cover the exact regression where search, index rebuild, doctor fix, initialize, or write flows create .maat in the product repo because storage fell back to cwd.

## Acceptance

- A test runs a representative read/search command from a non-storage source repo with missing config and asserts no .maat directory is created.
- A test runs the same command with configured storage and asserts the cache lands under configured storage/.maat.
- The test suite covers both human and JSON error paths where practical.

documents.title: Ticket: Guard index writes to resolved storage
documents.content:
# Ticket: Guard index writes to resolved storage

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260529-141814-2980 |
| Project | maat |
| Goal | G-20260529-141740-65d8 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-29T14:18:14+02:00 |

## Description

Make index refresh code write only under the already resolved storage root and fail loudly when storage resolution is absent or ambiguous.

## Acceptance

- WriteIndex and SQLite rebuild callers receive an explicit resolved storage path.
- Index refresh never independently falls back to cwd.
- Failures report that indexes are rebuildable and identify the intended storage path when available.

documents.title: Ticket: Document configured storage and cache boundaries
documents.content:
# Ticket: Document configured storage and cache boundaries

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260529-141820-ecec |
| Project | maat |
| Goal | G-20260529-141740-65d8 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-29T14:18:20+02:00 |

## Description

Update user and agent documentation so configured storage is the normal operating mode and cache files are described as storage-local rebuildable artifacts.

## Acceptance

- README and install/search docs explain that normal commands use configured storage after setup.
- Docs state that <storage>/.maat/index.json and <storage>/.maat/index.sqlite are rebuildable and not committed.
- Docs no longer imply source repos may safely receive .maat caches during normal use.

documents.title: Ticket: Decide and enforce storage cache ignore policy
documents.content:
# Ticket: Decide and enforce storage cache ignore policy

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260529-142348-4b56 |
| Project | maat |
| Goal | G-20260529-141740-65d8 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-29T14:23:48+02:00 |

## Description

Decide whether storage-local .maat cache files are only excluded by Maat sync pathspecs or also ignored by the storage repo itself, then make tooling and docs match that decision.

## Acceptance

- The project explicitly decides whether setup should create or maintain a storage .gitignore entry for .maat/.
- If .gitignore enforcement is chosen, setup or setup doctor creates or validates the ignore entry without touching product repos.
- If pathspec-only exclusion is chosen, docs and tests state that normal Git commands can still see .maat as local untracked or modified cache files.

# Ticket: Implement Core Agent token lifecycle API

| Field | Value |

## Description

Implement the Core side of Agent token lifecycle controls from docs/architecture/agent-token-lifecycle.md: token hash/version/revocation metadata, admin-authenticated rotate revoke reissue and status actions, revoked-token auth rejection, registration safeguards for revoked machine_id recovery, one-time token responses, redaction, audit events, and preserved Agent/monitor identity.

## Acceptance

- Core migrations, services, API handlers, route annotations, OpenAPI, and tests cover rotate revoke reissue and status; old tokens are rejected after rotation; revoked Agents cannot recover a token through unauthenticated /v1/register; agent_id machine_id monitor IDs reports incidents and maintenance state are preserved; normal Agent and frontend responses do not expose plaintext tokens.

## Acceptance
